definitions.zh: 退出状态观测表示退出状态被观察并暂存为智能体步骤上下文证据后的形式。
definitions.ja: 終了状態観測は、終了状態が観測され、エージェント手順のコンテキスト証拠としてステージングされた状態を表します。

why_needed.zh: 退出状态观测为canonical 事实 CommandOutputObservation-has_exit_status_observation-ExitStatusObservation（CommandOutputObservation has_exit_status_observation ExitStatusObservation）提供明确端点并保持与 CommandOutputObservation 的定义边界；节点字段 exit_status 使该区别可验证。
why_needed.en: exit status observation provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact CommandOutputObservation-has_exit_status_observation-ExitStatusObservation (CommandOutputObservation has_exit_status_observation ExitStatusObservation) while preserving its reviewed boundary from CommandOutputObservation; node fields exit_status make that distinction testable.
why_needed.ja: 終了状態観測はcanonical 事実 CommandOutputObservation-has_exit_status_observation-ExitStatusObservation（CommandOutputObservation has_exit_status_observation ExitStatusObservation）の明確な端点を提供し、CommandOutputObservation との審査済み境界を保ちながら。ノード項目 exit_status によりその区別を検証できます。

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of ExitStatusObservation: Every ExitStatusObservation is an ExecutionObservation of an execution's termination outcome, with the concrete status stored as a field. The ontology-specific boundary for ExitStatusObservation is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Specification > Architecture, base protocol, and server features

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of ExitStatusObservation: Every ExitStatusObservation is an ExecutionObservation of an execution's termination outcome, with the concrete status stored as a field. The ontology-specific boundary for ExitStatusObservation is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation page > Deep Agents JS docs heading and documented conceptual model

labels.ja: 標準エラーストリーム

short_definitions.zh: 标准错误记录命令、进程、沙箱或工具执行产生的诊断输出流，这些诊断输出可作为可观察证据进入上下文。
short_definitions.en: captures the stderr stream emitted by a command, process, sandbox, or tool execution as observable diagnostic output.
short_definitions.ja: 標準エラーは、コマンド、プロセス、サンドボックス、ツール実行から生じる診断出力ストリームを記録し、それらを観測可能な証拠としてコンテキストへ入れられるようにします。

definitions.en: captures the stderr stream emitted by a command, process, sandbox, or tool execution as observable diagnostic output.
definitions.zh: 标准错误记录命令、进程、沙箱或工具执行产生的诊断输出流，这些诊断输出可作为可观察证据进入上下文。
definitions.ja: 標準エラーは、コマンド、プロセス、サンドボックス、ツール実行から生じる診断出力ストリームを記録し、それらを観測可能な証拠としてコンテキストへ入れられるようにします。

why_needed.zh: 标准错误流为canonical 事实 StandardErrorChunk-part_of-StandardError（StandardErrorChunk part_of StandardError）提供明确端点并保持与 StandardOutput 的定义边界。
why_needed.en: standard error stream provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact StandardErrorChunk-part_of-StandardError (StandardErrorChunk part_of StandardError) while preserving its reviewed boundary from StandardOutput.
why_needed.ja: 標準エラーストリームはcanonical 事実 StandardErrorChunk-part_of-StandardError（StandardErrorChunk part_of StandardError）の明確な端点を提供し、StandardOutput との審査済み境界を保ちながら。

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of StandardError: Every stderr stream is an OutputStream ordered by production time and distinguished by its diagnostic channel. The ontology-specific boundary for StandardError is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Specification > Architecture, base protocol, and server features

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of StandardError: Every stderr stream is an OutputStream ordered by production time and distinguished by its diagnostic channel. The ontology-specific boundary for StandardError is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation page > Deep Agents JS docs heading and documented conceptual model

short_definitions.zh: 标准错误分块表示带有顺序、诊断含义、来源片段、截断和上下文选择元数据的有边界标准错误片段。
short_definitions.en: represents a bounded stderr segment with ordering, diagnostic meaning, source span, truncation, and context-selection metadata.
short_definitions.ja: 標準エラーチャンクは、順序、診断上の意味、出典断片、切り詰め、コンテキスト選択メタデータを持つ境界付きの標準エラー部分です。

definitions.en: represents a bounded stderr segment with ordering, diagnostic meaning, source span, truncation, and context-selection metadata.
definitions.zh: 标准错误分块表示带有顺序、诊断含义、来源片段、截断和上下文选择元数据的有边界标准错误片段。
definitions.ja: 標準エラーチャンクは、順序、診断上の意味、出典断片、切り詰め、コンテキスト選択メタデータを持つ境界付きの標準エラー部分です。

why_needed.zh: 标准错误分块为canonical 事实 StandardErrorChunk-part_of-StandardError（StandardErrorChunk part_of StandardError）提供明确端点并保持与 StandardOutputChunk 的定义边界。
why_needed.en: standard error chunk provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact StandardErrorChunk-part_of-StandardError (StandardErrorChunk part_of StandardError) while preserving its reviewed boundary from StandardOutputChunk.
why_needed.ja: 標準エラーチャンクはcanonical 事実 StandardErrorChunk-part_of-StandardError（StandardErrorChunk part_of StandardError）の明確な端点を提供し、StandardOutputChunk との審査済み境界を保ちながら。

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of StandardErrorChunk: Every StandardErrorChunk is an OutputChunk whose source channel is stderr. The ontology-specific boundary for StandardErrorChunk is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Specification > Architecture, base protocol, and server features

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of StandardErrorChunk: Every StandardErrorChunk is an OutputChunk whose source channel is stderr. The ontology-specific boundary for StandardErrorChunk is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation page > Deep Agents JS docs heading and documented conceptual model

short_definitions.zh: 标准输出捕获命令、进程、沙箱或工具执行发出的标准输出流，作为可暂存进上下文的可观察输出。
short_definitions.en: captures the stdout stream emitted by a command, process, sandbox, or tool execution as observable output available for staging into context.
short_definitions.ja: 標準出力は、コマンド、プロセス、サンドボックス、ツール実行から発せられる出力ストリームを、コンテキストへステージング可能な観測出力として捕捉します。

definitions.en: captures the stdout stream emitted by a command, process, sandbox, or tool execution as observable output available for staging into context.
definitions.zh: 标准输出捕获命令、进程、沙箱或工具执行发出的标准输出流，作为可暂存进上下文的可观察输出。
definitions.ja: 標準出力は、コマンド、プロセス、サンドボックス、ツール実行から発せられる出力ストリームを、コンテキストへステージング可能な観測出力として捕捉します。

why_needed.zh: 标准输出流为canonical 事实 StandardOutputChunk-part_of-StandardOutput（StandardOutputChunk part_of StandardOutput）提供明确端点并保持与 StandardError 的定义边界。
why_needed.en: standard output stream provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact StandardOutputChunk-part_of-StandardOutput (StandardOutputChunk part_of StandardOutput) while preserving its reviewed boundary from StandardError.
why_needed.ja: 標準出力ストリームはcanonical 事実 StandardOutputChunk-part_of-StandardOutput（StandardOutputChunk part_of StandardOutput）の明確な端点を提供し、StandardError との審査済み境界を保ちながら。

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of StandardOutput: Every stdout stream is an OutputStream ordered by production time and distinguished by its regular-output channel. The ontology-specific boundary for StandardOutput is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Specification > Architecture, base protocol, and server features

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of StandardOutput: Every stdout stream is an OutputStream ordered by production time and distinguished by its regular-output channel. The ontology-specific boundary for StandardOutput is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation page > Deep Agents JS docs heading and documented conceptual model

short_definitions.zh: 标准输出分块表示带有顺序、来源片段、截断和上下文选择元数据的有边界标准输出片段。
short_definitions.en: represents a bounded stdout segment with ordering, source span, truncation, and context-selection metadata.
short_definitions.ja: 標準出力チャンクは、順序、出典断片、切り詰め、コンテキスト選択メタデータを持つ境界付きの標準出力部分です。

definitions.en: represents a bounded stdout segment with ordering, source span, truncation, and context-selection metadata.
definitions.zh: 标准输出分块表示带有顺序、来源片段、截断和上下文选择元数据的有边界标准输出片段。
definitions.ja: 標準出力チャンクは、順序、出典断片、切り詰め、コンテキスト選択メタデータを持つ境界付きの標準出力部分です。

why_needed.zh: 标准输出分块为canonical 事实 StandardOutputChunk-part_of-StandardOutput（StandardOutputChunk part_of StandardOutput）提供明确端点并保持与 StandardErrorChunk 的定义边界。
why_needed.en: standard output chunk provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact StandardOutputChunk-part_of-StandardOutput (StandardOutputChunk part_of StandardOutput) while preserving its reviewed boundary from StandardErrorChunk.
why_needed.ja: 標準出力チャンクはcanonical 事実 StandardOutputChunk-part_of-StandardOutput（StandardOutputChunk part_of StandardOutput）の明確な端点を提供し、StandardErrorChunk との審査済み境界を保ちながら。

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of StandardOutputChunk: Every StandardOutputChunk is an OutputChunk whose source channel is stdout. The ontology-specific boundary for StandardOutputChunk is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Specification > Architecture, base protocol, and server features

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of StandardOutputChunk: Every StandardOutputChunk is an OutputChunk whose source channel is stdout. The ontology-specific boundary for StandardOutputChunk is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation page > Deep Agents JS docs heading and documented conceptual model

labels.ja: 作業ディレクトリ参照

short_definitions.zh: 工作目录引用记录与观测执行输出相关的工作目录，而不把目录本身建模为上下文摄入产物。
short_definitions.en: references the working directory relevant to observed execution output without making the directory itself a context-ingress artifact.
short_definitions.ja: 作業ディレクトリ参照は、観測された実行出力に関係する作業ディレクトリを記録し、ディレクトリ自体をコンテキスト取り込み成果物としては扱いません。

definitions.en: references the working directory relevant to observed execution output without making the directory itself a context-ingress artifact.
definitions.zh: 工作目录引用记录与观测执行输出相关的工作目录，而不把目录本身建模为上下文摄入产物。
definitions.ja: 作業ディレクトリ参照は、観測された実行出力に関係する作業ディレクトリを記録し、ディレクトリ自体をコンテキスト取り込み成果物としては扱いません。

why_needed.zh: 工作目录引用为canonical 事实 WorkingDirectoryReference-is_a-ExecutionReference（WorkingDirectoryReference is_a ExecutionReference）提供明确端点并保持与 EnvironmentBindingReference 的定义边界。
why_needed.en: working directory reference provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact WorkingDirectoryReference-is_a-ExecutionReference (WorkingDirectoryReference is_a ExecutionReference) while preserving its reviewed boundary from EnvironmentBindingReference.
why_needed.ja: 作業ディレクトリ参照はcanonical 事実 WorkingDirectoryReference-is_a-ExecutionReference（WorkingDirectoryReference is_a ExecutionReference）の明確な端点を提供し、EnvironmentBindingReference との審査済み境界を保ちながら。

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of WorkingDirectoryReference: Every WorkingDirectoryReference is an ExecutionReference to the directory in which execution occurred, not the directory resource itself. The ontology-specific boundary for WorkingDirectoryReference is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Specification > Architecture, base protocol, and server features

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of WorkingDirectoryReference: Every WorkingDirectoryReference is an ExecutionReference to the directory in which execution occurred, not the directory resource itself. The ontology-specific boundary for WorkingDirectoryReference is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation page > Deep Agents JS docs heading and documented conceptual model

short_definitions.zh: 对一次执行的可见输出、状态或结果所作的可审计观测记录。
short_definitions.en: An auditable observation record of visible output, status, or result from an execution.
short_definitions.ja: 実行の可視出力・状態・結果に関する監査可能な観測記録。

definitions.zh: ExecutionObservation 是具有身份、观测时间、被观测执行引用、可见载荷和来源证据的信息对象；它只记录经允许可见的执行现象，不拥有命令或执行活动。
definitions.en: ExecutionObservation is identity-bearing information with observation time, a reference to the observed execution, visible payload, and source evidence. It records permitted observable execution phenomena without owning the command or execution activity.
definitions.ja: ExecutionObservation は識別・観測時刻・観測対象実行参照・可視ペイロード・出典証拠を持つ情報で、コマンドや実行活動を所有せず許可された実行現象だけを記録します。

why_needed.zh: 执行观测为canonical 事实 ExecutionObservation-observes-ExecutionResult（ExecutionObservation observes ExecutionResult）提供明确端点并保持与 ExecutionResult 的定义边界；节点字段 observation_id、observed_at 使该区别可验证。
why_needed.en: execution observation provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact ExecutionObservation-observes-ExecutionResult (ExecutionObservation observes ExecutionResult) while preserving its reviewed boundary from ExecutionResult; node fields observation_id, observed_at make that distinction testable.
why_needed.ja: 実行観測はcanonical 事実 ExecutionObservation-observes-ExecutionResult（ExecutionObservation observes ExecutionResult）の明確な端点を提供し、ExecutionResult との審査済み境界を保ちながら。ノード項目 observation_id、observed_at によりその区別を検証できます。

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of ExecutionObservation: OpenAI tracing records observable execution input/output as trace information, while MCP distinguishes request execution from returned results and errors; together they support a distinct observation-information genus. The ontology-specific boundary for ExecutionObservation is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Specification > Architecture, base protocol, and server features

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of ExecutionObservation: OpenAI tracing records observable execution input/output as trace information, while MCP distinguishes request execution from returned results and errors; together they support a distinct observation-information genus. The ontology-specific boundary for ExecutionObservation is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Tracing guide > Traces, spans, and trace processors

short_definitions.zh: 对执行结果、工作目录或环境绑定的稳定引用信息。
short_definitions.en: Stable reference information for an execution result, working directory, or environment binding.
short_definitions.ja: 実行結果・作業ディレクトリ・環境バインディングへの安定参照情報。

definitions.zh: ExecutionReference 是具有引用标识、引用种类、目标系统与可见性约束的信息对象，用于指向与执行有关的对象而不复制目标对象或泄露其隐藏值。
definitions.en: ExecutionReference is information with a reference identifier, reference kind, target system, and visibility constraints. It points to execution-related objects without copying them or exposing hidden values.
definitions.ja: ExecutionReference は参照識別子・参照種別・対象系・可視性制約を持ち、対象を複製したり隠れ値を漏らさず実行関連対象を指す情報です。

why_needed.zh: 执行引用为canonical 事实 EnvironmentBindingReference-is_a-ExecutionReference（EnvironmentBindingReference is_a ExecutionReference）提供明确端点并保持与 EnvironmentBindingReference 的定义边界。
why_needed.en: execution reference provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact EnvironmentBindingReference-is_a-ExecutionReference (EnvironmentBindingReference is_a ExecutionReference) while preserving its reviewed boundary from EnvironmentBindingReference.
why_needed.ja: 実行参照はcanonical 事実 EnvironmentBindingReference-is_a-ExecutionReference（EnvironmentBindingReference is_a ExecutionReference）の明確な端点を提供し、EnvironmentBindingReference との審査済み境界を保ちながら。

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of ExecutionReference: Protocol result identifiers and tracing references both require stable links to execution evidence without copying execution objects; this anchor groups reference information only. The ontology-specific boundary for ExecutionReference is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Specification > Architecture, base protocol, and server features

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of ExecutionReference: Protocol result identifiers and tracing references both require stable links to execution evidence without copying execution objects; this anchor groups reference information only. The ontology-specific boundary for ExecutionReference is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Tracing guide > Traces, spans, and trace processors

short_definitions.zh: 音频块表示带有模态和来源元数据、并暂存进上下文的音频内容或转写音频观测。
short_definitions.en: represents audio content or transcribed audio observations staged into context with modality and source metadata.
short_definitions.ja: 音声ブロックは、モダリティと出典メタデータを持ち、コンテキストへステージングされる音声内容または文字起こし観測を表します。

definitions.en: represents audio content or transcribed audio observations staged into context with modality and source metadata.
definitions.zh: 音频块表示带有模态和来源元数据、并暂存进上下文的音频内容或转写音频观测。
definitions.ja: 音声ブロックは、モダリティと出典メタデータを持ち、コンテキストへステージングされる音声内容または文字起こし観測を表します。

why_needed.zh: 音频内容块为canonical 事实 AudioBlock-transcribed_as-TextBlock（AudioBlock transcribed_as TextBlock）提供明确端点并保持与 CodeBlock 的定义边界；节点字段 modality、block_id、mime_type 使该区别可验证。
why_needed.en: audio block provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact AudioBlock-transcribed_as-TextBlock (AudioBlock transcribed_as TextBlock) while preserving its reviewed boundary from CodeBlock; node fields modality, block_id, mime_type make that distinction testable.
why_needed.ja: 音声内容ブロックはcanonical 事実 AudioBlock-transcribed_as-TextBlock（AudioBlock transcribed_as TextBlock）の明確な端点を提供し、CodeBlock との審査済み境界を保ちながら。ノード項目 modality、block_id、mime_type によりその区別を検証できます。

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of AudioBlock: Every AudioBlock is a ContentBlock whose payload modality is audio or an audio observation. The ontology-specific boundary for AudioBlock is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Specification > Architecture, base protocol, and server features

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of AudioBlock: Every AudioBlock is a ContentBlock whose payload modality is audio or an audio observation. The ontology-specific boundary for AudioBlock is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation page > Deep Agents JS docs heading and documented conceptual model

short_definitions.zh: 代码块表示暂存进上下文的源码、补丁、堆栈轨迹、壳层片段或可执行文本。
short_definitions.en: represents source code, patches, stack traces, shell snippets, or executable text staged into context.
short_definitions.ja: コードブロックは、ソースコード、パッチ、スタックトレース、シェル断片、実行可能テキストなど、コンテキストへステージングされる内容を表します。

definitions.en: represents source code, patches, stack traces, shell snippets, or executable text staged into context.
definitions.zh: 代码块表示暂存进上下文的源码、补丁、堆栈轨迹、壳层片段或可执行文本。
definitions.ja: コードブロックは、ソースコード、パッチ、スタックトレース、シェル断片、実行可能テキストなど、コンテキストへステージングされる内容を表します。

why_needed.zh: 代码内容块为canonical 事实 CodeBlock-is_a-ContentBlock（CodeBlock is_a ContentBlock）提供明确端点并保持与 AudioBlock 的定义边界；节点字段 modality、block_id、mime_type 使该区别可验证。
why_needed.en: code block provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact CodeBlock-is_a-ContentBlock (CodeBlock is_a ContentBlock) while preserving its reviewed boundary from AudioBlock; node fields modality, block_id, mime_type make that distinction testable.
why_needed.ja: コード内容ブロックはcanonical 事実 CodeBlock-is_a-ContentBlock（CodeBlock is_a ContentBlock）の明確な端点を提供し、AudioBlock との審査済み境界を保ちながら。ノード項目 modality、block_id、mime_type によりその区別を検証できます。

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of CodeBlock: Every CodeBlock is a ContentBlock interpreted as source code, a patch, a stack trace, or command syntax. The ontology-specific boundary for CodeBlock is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Specification > Architecture, base protocol, and server features

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of CodeBlock: Every CodeBlock is a ContentBlock interpreted as source code, a patch, a stack trace, or command syntax. The ontology-specific boundary for CodeBlock is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation page > Deep Agents JS docs heading and documented conceptual model

short_definitions.zh: 内容块表示带有模态、编码、来源引用、令牌预算和可见性元数据的有边界上下文内容单元。
short_definitions.en: represents a bounded unit of context content with modality, encoding, source reference, token budget, and visibility metadata.
short_definitions.ja: 内容ブロックは、モダリティ、符号化、出典参照、トークン予算、可視性メタデータを持つ境界付きコンテキスト内容単位です。

definitions.en: represents a bounded unit of context content with modality, encoding, source reference, token budget, and visibility metadata.
definitions.zh: 内容块表示带有模态、编码、来源引用、令牌预算和可见性元数据的有边界上下文内容单元。
definitions.ja: 内容ブロックは、モダリティ、符号化、出典参照、トークン予算、可視性メタデータを持つ境界付きコンテキスト内容単位です。

why_needed.zh: 内容块为canonical 事实 compressed_into_summary（ContentBlock compressed_into_summary ContextSummary）提供明确端点并保持与 ContextSummary 的定义边界；节点字段 modality、mime_type、block_id 使该区别可验证。
why_needed.en: content block provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact compressed_into_summary (ContentBlock compressed_into_summary ContextSummary) while preserving its reviewed boundary from ContextSummary; node fields modality, mime_type, block_id make that distinction testable.
why_needed.ja: 内容ブロックはcanonical 事実 compressed_into_summary（ContentBlock compressed_into_summary ContextSummary）の明確な端点を提供し、ContextSummary との審査済み境界を保ちながら。ノード項目 modality、mime_type、block_id によりその区別を検証できます。

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of ContentBlock: ContentBlock is the most general bounded content unit with payload, source, and visibility information, reusable by messages, context packages, and outputs. The ontology-specific boundary for ContentBlock is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Specification > Architecture, base protocol, and server features

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of ContentBlock: ContentBlock is the most general bounded content unit with payload, source, and visibility information, reusable by messages, context packages, and outputs. The ontology-specific boundary for ContentBlock is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation page > Deep Agents JS docs heading and documented conceptual model

labels.ja: ファイル添付内容ブロック

short_definitions.zh: 文件附件块表示对模型调用可见的附加文件或文件摘录，并记录摘要、媒体类型和访问引用。
short_definitions.en: represents an attached file or file excerpt made visible to a model call, including digest, mime type, and access reference.
short_definitions.ja: ファイル添付ブロックは、モデル呼び出しに見える添付ファイルまたはファイル抜粋を表し、ダイジェスト、メディア種別、アクセス参照を記録します。

definitions.en: represents an attached file or file excerpt made visible to a model call, including digest, mime type, and access reference.

why_needed.zh: 文件附件内容块为canonical 事实 FileAttachmentBlock-references_source-SourceReference（FileAttachmentBlock references_source SourceReference）提供明确端点并保持与 AudioBlock 的定义边界；节点字段 modality、block_id、mime_type 使该区别可验证。

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of FileAttachmentBlock: Every FileAttachmentBlock is a ContentBlock whose payload is a file reference or file excerpt. source_claims.locator: Specification > Architecture, base protocol, and server features

definitions.zh: 候选排名是特定发现运行中对候选顺序位置的观测质量，不是索引、结果或候选的子类；保留概念以承载方法与证据。

short_definitions.en: points from a context selection process to an index entry, search surface, or retrieval handle that may produce candidate content.
short_definitions.ja: 索引ポインタは、コンテキスト選択過程を、候補内容を生み得る索引項目、検索面、検索ハンドルへ指し示します。

definitions.en: points from a context selection process to an index entry, search surface, or retrieval handle that may produce candidate content.

short_definitions.zh: 轻量检索轨迹记录一次轻量上下文发现过程中的查询、候选、评分、排序和来源引用证据。
short_definitions.en: records observable query, candidate, score, rank, and source-reference evidence for a lightweight context discovery pass.
short_definitions.ja: 軽量検索トレースは、軽量なコンテキスト発見における問い合わせ、候補、スコア、順位、出典参照証拠を記録します。

definitions.en: records observable query, candidate, score, rank, and source-reference evidence for a lightweight context discovery pass.
definitions.zh: 轻量检索轨迹记录一次轻量上下文发现过程中的查询、候选、评分、排序和来源引用证据。
definitions.ja: 軽量検索トレースは、軽量なコンテキスト発見における問い合わせ、候補、スコア、順位、出典参照証拠を記録します。

why_needed.zh: 轻量检索轨迹为canonical 事实 LightweightRetrievalTrace-records-DiscoveryResult（LightweightRetrievalTrace records DiscoveryResult）提供明确端点并保持与 DiscoveryResult 的定义边界。
why_needed.en: lightweight retrieval trace provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact LightweightRetrievalTrace-records-DiscoveryResult (LightweightRetrievalTrace records DiscoveryResult) while preserving its reviewed boundary from DiscoveryResult.
why_needed.ja: 軽量検索トレースはcanonical 事実 LightweightRetrievalTrace-records-DiscoveryResult（LightweightRetrievalTrace records DiscoveryResult）の明確な端点を提供し、DiscoveryResult との審査済み境界を保ちながら。

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of LightweightRetrievalTrace: It is replayable information recording query, candidates, scores, ranks, and source evidence, not a subtype of discovery result or index. The ontology-specific boundary for LightweightRetrievalTrace is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Specification > Architecture, base protocol, and server features

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of LightweightRetrievalTrace: It is replayable information recording query, candidates, scores, ranks, and source evidence, not a subtype of discovery result or index. The ontology-specific boundary for LightweightRetrievalTrace is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation page > Deep Agents JS docs heading and documented conceptual model

short_definitions.zh: 检索评分记录附加在检索上下文候选上的相关性、置信度、相似度、词汇匹配或重排证据。
short_definitions.en: records relevance, confidence, similarity, lexical match, or rerank evidence attached to a retrieved context candidate.
short_definitions.ja: 検索スコアは、検索コンテキスト候補に付く関連度、信頼度、類似度、語彙一致、再順位付け根拠を記録します。

definitions.en: records relevance, confidence, similarity, lexical match, or rerank evidence attached to a retrieved context candidate.
definitions.zh: 检索评分记录附加在检索上下文候选上的相关性、置信度、相似度、词汇匹配或重排证据。
definitions.ja: 検索スコアは、検索コンテキスト候補に付く関連度、信頼度、類似度、語彙一致、再順位付け根拠を記録します。

why_needed.zh: 检索评分为canonical 事实 CandidateChunk-scored_by-RetrievalScore（CandidateChunk scored_by RetrievalScore）提供明确端点并保持与 SearchScore 的定义边界。
why_needed.en: retrieval score provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact CandidateChunk-scored_by-RetrievalScore (CandidateChunk scored_by RetrievalScore) while preserving its reviewed boundary from SearchScore.
why_needed.ja: 検索スコアはcanonical 事実 CandidateChunk-scored_by-RetrievalScore（CandidateChunk scored_by RetrievalScore）の明確な端点を提供し、SearchScore との審査済み境界を保ちながら。

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of RetrievalScore: Every RetrievalScore is a DiscoveryScore produced by a similarity, relevance, or reranking method. The ontology-specific boundary for RetrievalScore is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Specification > Architecture, base protocol, and server features

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of RetrievalScore: Every RetrievalScore is a DiscoveryScore produced by a similarity, relevance, or reranking method. The ontology-specific boundary for RetrievalScore is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation page > Deep Agents JS docs heading and documented conceptual model

short_definitions.zh: 检索候选表示系统决定是否纳入上下文包之前，由检索返回的候选内容。
short_definitions.en: represents content returned by retrieval before the system decides whether it enters the context package.
short_definitions.ja: 検索候補は、コンテキストパッケージに入れるかどうかを決める前に検索から返された候補内容を表します。

definitions.en: represents content returned by retrieval before the system decides whether it enters the context package.
definitions.zh: 检索候选表示系统决定是否纳入上下文包之前，由检索返回的候选内容。
definitions.ja: 検索候補は、コンテキストパッケージに入れるかどうかを決める前に検索から返された候補内容を表します。

why_needed.zh: 检索候选为canonical 事实 RetrievedCandidate-ranked_at-CandidateRank（RetrievedCandidate ranked_at CandidateRank）提供明确端点并保持与 SearchResult 的定义边界。
why_needed.en: retrieved candidate provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact RetrievedCandidate-ranked_at-CandidateRank (RetrievedCandidate ranked_at CandidateRank) while preserving its reviewed boundary from SearchResult.
why_needed.ja: 検索候補はcanonical 事実 RetrievedCandidate-ranked_at-CandidateRank（RetrievedCandidate ranked_at CandidateRank）の明確な端点を提供し、SearchResult との審査済み境界を保ちながら。

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of RetrievedCandidate: Every RetrievedCandidate is a DiscoveryResult returned by discovery and still awaiting selection. The ontology-specific boundary for RetrievedCandidate is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Specification > Architecture, base protocol, and server features

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of RetrievedCandidate: Every RetrievedCandidate is a DiscoveryResult returned by discovery and still awaiting selection. The ontology-specific boundary for RetrievedCandidate is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation page > Deep Agents JS docs heading and documented conceptual model

labels.ja: 検索コンテキスト候補

short_definitions.zh: 检索上下文候选表示专门针对某次模型调用或智能体步骤上下文包进行纳入评估的检索候选。
short_definitions.en: represents a retrieved candidate evaluated specifically for inclusion in a model-call or agent-step context package.
short_definitions.ja: 検索コンテキスト候補は、特定のモデル呼び出しまたはエージェント手順のコンテキストパッケージへ入れるか評価される検索候補を表します。

definitions.en: represents a retrieved candidate evaluated specifically for inclusion in a model-call or agent-step context package.
definitions.zh: 检索上下文候选表示专门针对某次模型调用或智能体步骤上下文包进行纳入评估的检索候选。
definitions.ja: 検索コンテキスト候補は、特定のモデル呼び出しまたはエージェント手順のコンテキストパッケージへ入れるか評価される検索候補を表します。

why_needed.zh: 检索上下文候选为canonical 事实 RetrievedContextCandidate-scored_by-RetrievalScore（RetrievedContextCandidate scored_by RetrievalScore）提供明确端点并保持与 RetrievedCandidate 的定义边界。
why_needed.en: retrieved context candidate provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact RetrievedContextCandidate-scored_by-RetrievalScore (RetrievedContextCandidate scored_by RetrievalScore) while preserving its reviewed boundary from RetrievedCandidate.
why_needed.ja: 検索コンテキスト候補はcanonical 事実 RetrievedContextCandidate-scored_by-RetrievalScore（RetrievedContextCandidate scored_by RetrievalScore）の明確な端点を提供し、RetrievedCandidate との審査済み境界を保ちながら。

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of RetrievedContextCandidate: Every RetrievedContextCandidate is a RetrievedCandidate evaluated for inclusion in a concrete ContextPackage. The ontology-specific boundary for RetrievedContextCandidate is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Specification > Architecture, base protocol, and server features

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of RetrievedContextCandidate: Every RetrievedContextCandidate is a RetrievedCandidate evaluated for inclusion in a concrete ContextPackage. The ontology-specific boundary for RetrievedContextCandidate is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation page > Deep Agents JS docs heading and documented conceptual model

short_definitions.zh: 搜索结果记录搜索或发现表面返回的候选项，包括来源引用、评分、排序和选择状态。
short_definitions.en: records a candidate returned by a search or discovery surface, including source reference, score, rank, and selection status.
short_definitions.ja: 検索結果は、検索または発見面から返された候補を記録し、出典参照、スコア、順位、選択状態を含みます。

definitions.en: records a candidate returned by a search or discovery surface, including source reference, score, rank, and selection status.
definitions.zh: 搜索结果记录搜索或发现表面返回的候选项，包括来源引用、评分、排序和选择状态。
definitions.ja: 検索結果は、検索または発見面から返された候補を記録し、出典参照、スコア、順位、選択状態を含みます。

why_needed.zh: 搜索结果为canonical 事实 SearchResult-scored_by-SearchScore（SearchResult scored_by SearchScore）提供明确端点并保持与 RetrievedCandidate 的定义边界。
why_needed.en: search result provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact SearchResult-scored_by-SearchScore (SearchResult scored_by SearchScore) while preserving its reviewed boundary from RetrievedCandidate.
why_needed.ja: 検索結果はcanonical 事実 SearchResult-scored_by-SearchScore（SearchResult scored_by SearchScore）の明確な端点を提供し、RetrievedCandidate との審査済み境界を保ちながら。

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of SearchResult: Every SearchResult is a DiscoveryResult returned by a search surface with source and score evidence. The ontology-specific boundary for SearchResult is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Specification > Architecture, base protocol, and server features

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of SearchResult: Every SearchResult is a DiscoveryResult returned by a search surface with source and score evidence. The ontology-specific boundary for SearchResult is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation page > Deep Agents JS docs heading and documented conceptual model

short_definitions.zh: 搜索评分记录检索候选、搜索结果、索引命中或来源指针在上下文发现过程中的相关性、置信度或排序分数。
short_definitions.en: records the score attached to a retrieved candidate, search result, index hit, or source pointer during context discovery.
short_definitions.ja: 検索スコアは、コンテキスト発見過程における検索候補、検索結果、索引ヒット、出典ポインタの関連度、信頼度、順位付けスコアを記録します。

definitions.en: records the score attached to a retrieved candidate, search result, index hit, or source pointer during context discovery.
definitions.zh: 搜索评分记录检索候选、搜索结果、索引命中或来源指针在上下文发现过程中的相关性、置信度或排序分数。
definitions.ja: 検索スコアは、コンテキスト発見過程における検索候補、検索結果、索引ヒット、出典ポインタの関連度、信頼度、順位付けスコアを記録します。

why_needed.zh: 搜索评分为canonical 事实 SearchResult-scored_by-SearchScore（SearchResult scored_by SearchScore）提供明确端点并保持与 RetrievalScore 的定义边界。
why_needed.en: search score provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact SearchResult-scored_by-SearchScore (SearchResult scored_by SearchScore) while preserving its reviewed boundary from RetrievalScore.
why_needed.ja: 検索評価値はcanonical 事実 SearchResult-scored_by-SearchScore（SearchResult scored_by SearchScore）の明確な端点を提供し、RetrievalScore との審査済み境界を保ちながら。

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of SearchScore: Every SearchScore is a DiscoveryScore assigned to a result by a search-matching method. The ontology-specific boundary for SearchScore is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Specification > Architecture, base protocol, and server features

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of SearchScore: Every SearchScore is a DiscoveryScore assigned to a result by a search-matching method. The ontology-specific boundary for SearchScore is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation page > Deep Agents JS docs heading and documented conceptual model

short_definitions.zh: 为轻量内容发现提供可查询指针或条目集合的信息制品。
short_definitions.en: An information artifact providing queryable pointers or entries for lightweight content discovery.
short_definitions.ja: 軽量内容発見用の検索可能ポインタまたは項目集合を提供する情報成果物。

definitions.zh: DiscoveryIndex 是具有身份、构建或刷新时间、覆盖范围和版本引用的可查询信息制品，用于发现上下文候选；它不拥有 Memory 域持久索引的存储、分片或生命周期。
definitions.en: DiscoveryIndex is an identity-bearing, queryable information artifact with build or refresh time, coverage, and version reference for finding context candidates. It does not own Memory's persistent-index storage, shards, or lifecycle.
definitions.ja: DiscoveryIndex は識別・構築／更新時刻・範囲・版参照を持つ検索可能情報成果物で、コンテキスト候補を発見します。Memory の永続索引保存・シャード・ライフサイクルは所有しません。

why_needed.zh: 发现索引为canonical 事实 IndexPointer-references-DiscoveryIndex（IndexPointer references DiscoveryIndex）提供明确端点并保持与 IndexPointer 的定义边界。
why_needed.en: discovery index provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact IndexPointer-references-DiscoveryIndex (IndexPointer references DiscoveryIndex) while preserving its reviewed boundary from IndexPointer.
why_needed.ja: 発見インデックスはcanonical 事実 IndexPointer-references-DiscoveryIndex（IndexPointer references DiscoveryIndex）の明確な端点を提供し、IndexPointer との審査済み境界を保ちながら。

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of DiscoveryIndex: COMPASS context selection and Deep Agents' visible file/context discovery support a distinct lightweight discovery projection that is not a persistent retrieval backend. The ontology-specific boundary for DiscoveryIndex is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation page > Deep Agents JS docs heading and documented conceptual model

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of DiscoveryIndex: COMPASS context selection and Deep Agents' visible file/context discovery support a distinct lightweight discovery projection that is not a persistent retrieval backend. The ontology-specific boundary for DiscoveryIndex is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Paper > COMPASS: Enhancing Agent Long-Horizon Reasoning with Evolving Context; abstract and method or system-architecture sections

short_definitions.zh: 发现过程返回且可携带来源、评分和选择状态的信息结果。
short_definitions.en: Information returned by discovery with optional source, score, and selection status.
short_definitions.ja: 出典・スコア・選択状態を持ち得る発見処理の返却情報。

definitions.zh: DiscoveryResult 是具有结果身份、产生它的查询或界面引用、来源引用、可选评分与选择状态的信息对象；结果尚不意味着已进入 ContextPackage。
definitions.en: DiscoveryResult is information with result identity, a reference to the producing query or surface, source reference, optional score, and selection status. Being a result does not imply inclusion in a ContextPackage.
definitions.ja: DiscoveryResult は結果識別・生成クエリ／面参照・出典参照・任意スコア・選択状態を持つ情報で、結果であることは ContextPackage 採用を意味しません。

why_needed.zh: 发现结果为canonical 事实 LightweightRetrievalTrace-records-DiscoveryResult（LightweightRetrievalTrace records DiscoveryResult）提供明确端点并保持与 LightweightRetrievalTrace 的定义边界；节点字段 rank、result_id 使该区别可验证。
why_needed.en: discovery result provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact LightweightRetrievalTrace-records-DiscoveryResult (LightweightRetrievalTrace records DiscoveryResult) while preserving its reviewed boundary from LightweightRetrievalTrace; node fields rank, result_id make that distinction testable.
why_needed.ja: 発見結果はcanonical 事実 LightweightRetrievalTrace-records-DiscoveryResult（LightweightRetrievalTrace records DiscoveryResult）の明確な端点を提供し、LightweightRetrievalTrace との審査済み境界を保ちながら。ノード項目 rank、result_id によりその区別を検証できます。

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of DiscoveryResult: Context-retrieval systems distinguish returned candidates, scoring, and final selection; this anchor groups returned information objects only. The ontology-specific boundary for DiscoveryResult is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation page > Deep Agents JS docs heading and documented conceptual model

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of DiscoveryResult: Context-retrieval systems distinguish returned candidates, scoring, and final selection; this anchor groups returned information objects only. The ontology-specific boundary for DiscoveryResult is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Paper > COMPASS: Enhancing Agent Long-Horizon Reasoning with Evolving Context; abstract and method or system-architecture sections

short_definitions.zh: 发现方法针对候选或结果产生的带方法与尺度的观测质量。
short_definitions.en: An observed quality with method and scale produced by discovery for a candidate or result.
short_definitions.ja: 発見方法が候補または結果へ生成する方法・尺度付き観測品質。

definitions.zh: DiscoveryScore 是对发现结果在相关性、相似度、词法匹配或重排尺度上的观测质量，必须记录评分方法、尺度、方向、时间和被评分对象；它不是结果或索引。
definitions.en: DiscoveryScore is an observed quality of a discovery result on relevance, similarity, lexical-match, or reranking scales. It records method, scale, direction, time, and scored subject; it is neither a result nor an index.
definitions.ja: DiscoveryScore は発見結果の関連性・類似度・語彙一致・再順位尺度における観測品質で、方法・尺度・方向・時刻・対象を記録し、結果や索引ではありません。

why_needed.zh: 发现评分为canonical 事实 RetrievalScore-is_a-DiscoveryScore（RetrievalScore is_a DiscoveryScore）提供明确端点并保持与 RetrievalScore 的定义边界。
why_needed.en: discovery score provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact RetrievalScore-is_a-DiscoveryScore (RetrievalScore is_a DiscoveryScore) while preserving its reviewed boundary from RetrievalScore.
why_needed.ja: 発見スコアはcanonical 事実 RetrievalScore-is_a-DiscoveryScore（RetrievalScore is_a DiscoveryScore）の明確な端点を提供し、RetrievalScore との審査済み境界を保ちながら。

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of DiscoveryScore: Retrieval and context-selection evidence treat scoring as an observed property of candidates rather than a candidate category, requiring a separate quality genus. The ontology-specific boundary for DiscoveryScore is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation page > Deep Agents JS docs heading and documented conceptual model

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of DiscoveryScore: Retrieval and context-selection evidence treat scoring as an observed property of candidates rather than a candidate category, requiring a separate quality genus. The ontology-specific boundary for DiscoveryScore is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Paper > COMPASS: Enhancing Agent Long-Horizon Reasoning with Evolving Context; abstract and method or system-architecture sections

labels.ja: アシスタントメッセージ

short_definitions.zh: 助手消息记录由助手产生的消息包络，可包含模型输出、工具调用意图、引用或对外披露的推理摘要。
short_definitions.en: records an assistant-authored message envelope that may contain model output, tool-call intent, citations, or disclosed reasoning summaries.
short_definitions.ja: アシスタントメッセージは、モデル出力、ツール呼び出し意図、引用、外部に開示された推論要約を含み得る、アシスタント発のメッセージ包絡です。

definitions.en: records an assistant-authored message envelope that may contain model output, tool-call intent, citations, or disclosed reasoning summaries.
definitions.zh: 助手消息记录由助手产生的消息包络，可包含模型输出、工具调用意图、引用或对外披露的推理摘要。
definitions.ja: アシスタントメッセージは、モデル出力、ツール呼び出し意図、引用、外部に開示された推論要約を含み得る、アシスタント発のメッセージ包絡です。

why_needed.zh: 助手消息为canonical 事实 AssistantMessage-is_a-Message（AssistantMessage is_a Message）提供明确端点并保持与 DeveloperMessage 的定义边界；节点字段 message_role、sender_id、receiver_id 使该区别可验证。
why_needed.en: assistant message provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact AssistantMessage-is_a-Message (AssistantMessage is_a Message) while preserving its reviewed boundary from DeveloperMessage; node fields message_role, sender_id, receiver_id make that distinction testable.
why_needed.ja: アシスタントメッセージはcanonical 事実 AssistantMessage-is_a-Message（AssistantMessage is_a Message）の明確な端点を提供し、DeveloperMessage との審査済み境界を保ちながら。ノード項目 message_role、sender_id、receiver_id によりその区別を検証できます。

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of AssistantMessage: Every AssistantMessage is a Message whose sending role is assistant and may carry model output or tool intent. The ontology-specific boundary for AssistantMessage is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Specification > Architecture, base protocol, and server features

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of AssistantMessage: Every AssistantMessage is a Message whose sending role is assistant and may carry model output or tool intent. The ontology-specific boundary for AssistantMessage is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation page > Deep Agents JS docs heading and documented conceptual model

labels.ja: コンテキストパッケージ

short_definitions.zh: 上下文包表示一次模型调用或智能体步骤实际可见的消息包络、指令、内容块、来源引用、检索候选、工具观测、引用、排除项和预算元数据。
short_definitions.en: bundles the exact message envelopes, instructions, content blocks, source references, retrieval candidates, tool observations, citations, exclusions, and budget metadata visible to one model call or agent step.
short_definitions.ja: コンテキストパッケージは、単一のモデル呼び出しまたはエージェント手順が実際に参照するメッセージ包絡、指示、内容ブロック、出典参照、検索候補、ツール観測、引用、除外項目、予算メタデータをまとめます。

definitions.en: bundles the exact message envelopes, instructions, content blocks, source references, retrieval candidates, tool observations, citations, exclusions, and budget metadata visible to one model call or agent step.
definitions.zh: 上下文包表示一次模型调用或智能体步骤实际可见的消息包络、指令、内容块、来源引用、检索候选、工具观测、引用、排除项和预算元数据。
definitions.ja: コンテキストパッケージは、単一のモデル呼び出しまたはエージェント手順が実際に参照するメッセージ包絡、指示、内容ブロック、出典参照、検索候補、ツール観測、引用、除外項目、予算メタデータをまとめます。

why_needed.zh: 上下文包为canonical 事实 ContextPackage-contains_candidate-RetrievedCandidate（ContextPackage contains_candidate RetrievedCandidate）提供明确端点并保持与 RetrievedCandidate 的定义边界。
why_needed.en: context package provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact ContextPackage-contains_candidate-RetrievedCandidate (ContextPackage contains_candidate RetrievedCandidate) while preserving its reviewed boundary from RetrievedCandidate.
why_needed.ja: コンテキストパッケージはcanonical 事実 ContextPackage-contains_candidate-RetrievedCandidate（ContextPackage contains_candidate RetrievedCandidate）の明確な端点を提供し、RetrievedCandidate との審査済み境界を保ちながら。

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of ContextPackage: ContextPackage is a versioned snapshot of information actually visible to one model call. Its sole canonical definition remains in Info; Memory only references it through produces or refreshes. The ontology-specific boundary for ContextPackage is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.

short_definitions.ja: ユーザーメッセージは、依頼内容、制約、修正、添付、フィードバックをコンテキストへ導入するユーザー発のメッセージ包絡です。

definitions.en: records a user-authored message envelope that introduces request content, constraints, corrections, attachments, or feedback into context.
definitions.zh: 用户消息记录由用户发出的消息包络，用于把请求内容、约束、修正、附件或反馈引入上下文。
definitions.ja: ユーザーメッセージは、依頼内容、制約、修正、添付、フィードバックをコンテキストへ導入するユーザー発のメッセージ包絡です。

why_needed.zh: 用户消息为canonical 事实 UserMessage-is_a-Message（UserMessage is_a Message）提供明确端点并保持与 AssistantMessage 的定义边界；节点字段 message_role、sender_id、receiver_id 使该区别可验证。
why_needed.en: user message provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact UserMessage-is_a-Message (UserMessage is_a Message) while preserving its reviewed boundary from AssistantMessage; node fields message_role, sender_id, receiver_id make that distinction testable.
why_needed.ja: ユーザーメッセージはcanonical 事実 UserMessage-is_a-Message（UserMessage is_a Message）の明確な端点を提供し、AssistantMessage との審査済み境界を保ちながら。ノード項目 message_role、sender_id、receiver_id によりその区別を検証できます。

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of UserMessage: Every UserMessage is a Message sent under the user role and introducing requests, constraints, attachments, or feedback. The ontology-specific boundary for UserMessage is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Specification > Architecture, base protocol, and server features

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of UserMessage: Every UserMessage is a Message sent under the user role and introducing requests, constraints, attachments, or feedback. The ontology-specific boundary for UserMessage is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.

short_definitions.zh: 用于组织会话整体、交换轮次或可观察消息历史的信息元素。
short_definitions.en: An information element organizing a conversation, exchange turn, or observable message history.
short_definitions.ja: 会話全体・交換ターン・観測可能メッセージ履歴を整理する情報要素。

definitions.zh: ConversationElement 是具有身份并参与会话结构组织的信息对象，其下位类分别覆盖完整会话、单个有序轮次和保留历史；它不规定发送角色或消息载荷。
definitions.en: ConversationElement is identity-bearing information participating in conversation structure. Its subtypes cover the full conversation, an ordered turn, and retained history; it does not prescribe sender role or message payload.
definitions.ja: ConversationElement は会話構造へ参加する識別付き情報で、下位型が会話全体・順序付きターン・保持履歴を覆い、送信役割やメッセージペイロードは規定しません。

why_needed.zh: 会话结构元素为canonical 事实 Conversation-is_a-ConversationElement（Conversation is_a ConversationElement）提供明确端点并保持与 Conversation 的定义边界。
why_needed.en: conversation structure element provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact Conversation-is_a-ConversationElement (Conversation is_a ConversationElement) while preserving its reviewed boundary from Conversation.
why_needed.ja: 会話構造要素はcanonical 事実 Conversation-is_a-ConversationElement（Conversation is_a ConversationElement）の明確な端点を提供し、Conversation との審査済み境界を保ちながら。

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of ConversationElement: FIPA ACL conversation/reply control and A2A message/task context both support modeling conversation structure separately from message payload. The ontology-specific boundary for ConversationElement is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Specification > Core concepts, Agent Card, Message, Task, and Artifact

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of ConversationElement: FIPA ACL conversation/reply control and A2A message/task context both support modeling conversation structure separately from message payload. The ontology-specific boundary for ConversationElement is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Specification > FIPA ACL Message Structure Specification; terminology, data model, and conformance sections

short_definitions.zh: 描述指令权威、优先级、范围、适用性或来源的可审计信息。
short_definitions.en: Auditable information describing an instruction's authority, priority, scope, applicability, or provenance.
short_definitions.ja: 指示の権限・優先度・範囲・適用性・来歴を記述する監査可能情報。

definitions.zh: InstructionMetadata 是与一个或多个 Instruction 关联、具有自己的来源和有效时间的信息对象，用于解释指令何时、为何、对谁以及以何种优先级生效；它不是指令内容本身。
definitions.en: InstructionMetadata is information associated with one or more Instructions and carrying its own provenance and validity time. It explains when, why, for whom, and at what precedence an instruction applies; it is not the directive content itself.
definitions.ja: InstructionMetadata は一つ以上の Instruction に関連し独自来歴と有効時刻を持つ情報で、いつ・なぜ・誰に・どの優先度で適用するかを説明し、指示内容自体ではありません。

why_needed.zh: 指令元数据为canonical 事实 InstructionApplicability-is_a-InstructionMetadata（InstructionApplicability is_a InstructionMetadata）提供明确端点并保持与 InstructionApplicability 的定义边界。
why_needed.en: instruction metadata provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact InstructionApplicability-is_a-InstructionMetadata (InstructionApplicability is_a InstructionMetadata) while preserving its reviewed boundary from InstructionApplicability.
why_needed.ja: 指示メタデータはcanonical 事実 InstructionApplicability-is_a-InstructionMetadata（InstructionApplicability is_a InstructionMetadata）の明確な端点を提供し、InstructionApplicability との審査済み境界を保ちながら。

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of InstructionMetadata: Major agent SDKs explicitly distinguish system/developer/user instruction authority and context scope, supporting a common metadata model separate from directive content. The ontology-specific boundary for InstructionMetadata is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation page > LangChain agents heading and documented conceptual model

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of InstructionMetadata: Major agent SDKs explicitly distinguish system/developer/user instruction authority and context scope, supporting a common metadata model separate from directive content. The ontology-specific boundary for InstructionMetadata is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation page > OpenAI Agents SDK Python docs heading and documented conceptual model

labels.ja: コンテキスト選択判断

short_definitions.zh: 这是记录候选被纳入、延后、抑制或排除之结果与理由的决定信息，不把一次决定误作一般事件类型。
short_definitions.en: This is decision information recording why a candidate was included, deferred, suppressed, or excluded, rather than treating the decision record as a generic event type.
short_definitions.ja: 候補の採用・延期・抑制・除外結果と理由を記録する判断情報で、一般イベント型とはしません。

definitions.zh: 这是记录候选被纳入、延后、抑制或排除之结果与理由的决定信息，不把一次决定误作一般事件类型。

short_definitions.zh: 已披露输出片段表示已经对接收方或下游上下文可见的输出段，并携带引用和边界元数据。
short_definitions.en: represents an output segment that has been made visible to a recipient or downstream context together with citation and boundary metadata.
short_definitions.ja: 開示済み出力セグメントは、受信者または下流コンテキストに見えるようになった出力部分を表し、引用と境界メタデータを持ちます。

definitions.en: represents an output segment that has been made visible to a recipient or downstream context together with citation and boundary metadata.

short_definitions.zh: 披露阶段记录输出或上下文内容被显示、保留、升级、脱敏或跨边界释放的步骤。
short_definitions.en: records a step in which output or context content is revealed, withheld, escalated, redacted, or released across a boundary.
short_definitions.ja: 開示段階は、出力またはコンテキスト内容が表示、保留、上位判断、秘匿化、境界越えの公開を受ける手順を記録します。

definitions.en: records a step in which output or context content is revealed, withheld, escalated, redacted, or released across a boundary.

why_needed.ja: 開示段階活動はcanonical 事実 DisclosureStage-governed_by-ProgressiveDisclosure（DisclosureStage governed_by ProgressiveDisclosure）の明確な端点を提供し、ProgressiveDisclosure との審査済み境界を保ちながら。

labels.ja: 先頭末尾出力ウィンドウ

short_definitions.zh: 首尾窗口保留长输出的开头和结尾片段，使关键设置和终端证据在长度限制下仍可见。
short_definitions.en: retains the beginning and ending spans of long output so important setup and terminal evidence can remain visible under length limits.
short_definitions.ja: 先頭末尾ウィンドウは、長い出力の冒頭と末尾の断片を保持し、重要な設定と終端証拠を長さ制限下でも見えるようにします。

definitions.en: retains the beginning and ending spans of long output so important setup and terminal evidence can remain visible under length limits.

short_definitions.zh: 输出分块表示命令、工具、模型、进程或产物输出中有边界的一段，用于引用、截断、披露或在后续步骤中暂存进上下文。

definitions.en: represents a bounded segment of command, tool, model, process, or artifact output prepared for citation, truncation, disclosure, or later context staging.

definitions.zh: 输出窗口标识可被显示、引用、摘要或传入后续上下文的输出保留范围。
definitions.ja: 出力ウィンドウは、表示、引用、要約、後続コンテキストへの受け渡しが可能な出力の保持範囲を識別します。

why_needed.zh: 输出窗口为canonical 事实 OutputWindow-selects_from-OutputStream（OutputWindow selects_from OutputStream）提供明确端点并保持与 OutputCitation 的定义边界；节点字段 length_limit、window_id、unit 使该区别可验证。
why_needed.en: output window provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact OutputWindow-selects_from-OutputStream (OutputWindow selects_from OutputStream) while preserving its reviewed boundary from OutputCitation; node fields length_limit, window_id, unit make that distinction testable.
why_needed.ja: 出力ウィンドウはcanonical 事実 OutputWindow-selects_from-OutputStream（OutputWindow selects_from OutputStream）の明確な端点を提供し、OutputCitation との審査済み境界を保ちながら。ノード項目 length_limit、window_id、unit によりその区別を検証できます。

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of OutputWindow: Every OutputWindow is an OutputInformation specification defining the range visible or retained for a recipient. The ontology-specific boundary for OutputWindow is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation page > Deep Agents JS docs heading and documented conceptual model

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of OutputWindow: Every OutputWindow is an OutputInformation specification defining the range visible or retained for a recipient. labels.ja: 抑制済みコンテキスト

short_definitions.zh: 被抑制上下文记录因策略、信任边界、相关性、隐私或预算限制而被有意排除在可见上下文之外的内容。
short_definitions.en: records content intentionally kept out of visible context because of policy, trust boundary, relevance, privacy, or budget constraints.
short_definitions.ja: 抑制済みコンテキストは、方針、信頼境界、関連度、プライバシー、予算制約により可視コンテキストから意図的に外された内容を記録します。

definitions.en: records content intentionally kept out of visible context because of policy, trust boundary, relevance, privacy, or budget constraints.
definitions.zh: 被抑制上下文记录因策略、信任边界、相关性、隐私或预算限制而被有意排除在可见上下文之外的内容。
definitions.ja: 抑制済みコンテキストは、方針、信頼境界、関連度、プライバシー、予算制約により可視コンテキストから意図的に外された内容を記録します。

why_needed.zh: 已抑制上下文为canonical 事实 suppressed_by_rule（SuppressedContext suppressed_by_rule DisclosureRule）提供明确端点并保持与 DisclosedOutputSegment 的定义边界。
why_needed.en: suppressed context provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact suppressed_by_rule (SuppressedContext suppressed_by_rule DisclosureRule) while preserving its reviewed boundary from DisclosedOutputSegment.
why_needed.ja: 抑制済みコンテキストはcanonical 事実 suppressed_by_rule（SuppressedContext suppressed_by_rule DisclosureRule）の明確な端点を提供し、DisclosedOutputSegment との審査済み境界を保ちながら。

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of SuppressedContext: Every SuppressedContext is a DisclosureArtifact recording content withheld for policy, privacy, relevance, or budget reasons. The ontology-specific boundary for SuppressedContext is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Specification > Architecture, base protocol, and server features

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of SuppressedContext: Every SuppressedContext is a DisclosureArtifact recording content withheld for policy, privacy, relevance, or budget reasons. The ontology-specific boundary for SuppressedContext is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.

labels.ja: 切詰め済みコンテキスト範囲

short_definitions.zh: 被截断上下文片段记录因上下文窗口、长度限制、摘要或输出裁剪决策而被移除或缩短的内容部分。
short_definitions.en: records the portion of content removed or shortened by a context window, length limit, summarization, or output clipping decision.
short_definitions.ja: 切り詰め済みコンテキスト断片は、コンテキストウィンドウ、長さ制限、要約、出力切り取り判断によって削除または短縮された内容部分を記録します。

definitions.en: records the portion of content removed or shortened by a context window, length limit, summarization, or output clipping decision.
definitions.zh: 被截断上下文片段记录因上下文窗口、长度限制、摘要或输出裁剪决策而被移除或缩短的内容部分。
definitions.ja: 切り詰め済みコンテキスト断片は、コンテキストウィンドウ、長さ制限、要約、出力切り取り判断によって削除または短縮された内容部分を記録します。

why_needed.zh: 已截断上下文片段为canonical 事实 TruncatedContextSpan-omitted_from-OutputWindow（TruncatedContextSpan omitted_from OutputWindow）提供明确端点并保持与 DisclosedOutputSegment 的定义边界。
why_needed.en: truncated context span provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact TruncatedContextSpan-omitted_from-OutputWindow (TruncatedContextSpan omitted_from OutputWindow) while preserving its reviewed boundary from DisclosedOutputSegment.
why_needed.ja: 切詰め済みコンテキスト範囲はcanonical 事実 TruncatedContextSpan-omitted_from-OutputWindow（TruncatedContextSpan omitted_from OutputWindow）の明確な端点を提供し、DisclosedOutputSegment との審査済み境界を保ちながら。

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of TruncatedContextSpan: Every TruncatedContextSpan is a DisclosureArtifact recording the extent removed by windowing, limits, or summarization, not a length value. The ontology-specific boundary for TruncatedContextSpan is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Specification > Architecture, base protocol, and server features

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of TruncatedContextSpan: Every TruncatedContextSpan is a DisclosureArtifact recording the extent removed by windowing, limits, or summarization, not a length value. The ontology-specific boundary for TruncatedContextSpan is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.

labels.ja: 可視コンテキストウィンドウ

short_definitions.zh: 可见上下文窗口表示经过选择、截断、抑制和披露控制后，特定参与者、模型调用或智能体步骤可见的内容范围。
short_definitions.en: represents the portion of content visible to a specific actor, model call, or agent step after selection, truncation, suppression, and disclosure controls.
short_definitions.ja: 可視コンテキストウィンドウは、選択、切り詰め、抑制、開示制御の後に、特定の参加者、モデル呼び出し、エージェント手順から見える内容範囲を表します。

definitions.en: represents the portion of content visible to a specific actor, model call, or agent step after selection, truncation, suppression, and disclosure controls.
definitions.zh: 可见上下文窗口表示经过选择、截断、抑制和披露控制后，特定参与者、模型调用或智能体步骤可见的内容范围。
definitions.ja: 可視コンテキストウィンドウは、選択、切り詰め、抑制、開示制御の後に、特定の参加者、モデル呼び出し、エージェント手順から見える内容範囲を表します。

why_needed.zh: 可见上下文窗口为canonical 事实 context_window_crosses_trust_boundary（VisibleContextWindow context_window_crosses_trust_boundary TrustBoundary）提供明确端点并保持与 HeadTailWindow 的定义边界。
why_needed.en: visible context window provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact context_window_crosses_trust_boundary (VisibleContextWindow context_window_crosses_trust_boundary TrustBoundary) while preserving its reviewed boundary from HeadTailWindow.
why_needed.ja: 可視コンテキストウィンドウはcanonical 事実 context_window_crosses_trust_boundary（VisibleContextWindow context_window_crosses_trust_boundary TrustBoundary）の明確な端点を提供し、HeadTailWindow との審査済み境界を保ちながら。

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of VisibleContextWindow: Every VisibleContextWindow is an OutputWindow defining the visible range for a specific Actor, model call, or step. The ontology-specific boundary for VisibleContextWindow is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Specification > Architecture, base protocol, and server features

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of VisibleContextWindow: Every VisibleContextWindow is an OutputWindow defining the visible range for a specific Actor, model call, or step. The ontology-specific boundary for VisibleContextWindow is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.

short_definitions.zh: 关于输出流、片段、窗口或其证据引用的可寻址信息。
short_definitions.en: Addressable information about an output stream, segment, window, or evidential citation.
short_definitions.ja: 出力ストリーム・区間・ウィンドウ・証拠引用に関するアドレス可能情報。

definitions.zh: OutputInformation 是具有来源、产生者、顺序或范围以及可见性信息的输出相关信息根；其下位类可以是流集合、地址化片段、可见范围规范或证据引用，但不是产生输出的活动。
definitions.en: OutputInformation is the root for output-related information with source, producer, order or extent, and visibility. Subtypes may be stream collections, addressable segments, visible-range specifications, or evidence citations, but not the activity producing output.
definitions.ja: OutputInformation は出典・生成者・順序／範囲・可視性を持つ出力関連情報の根で、下位型はストリーム集合・アドレス可能区間・可視範囲仕様・証拠引用となり得ますが、出力生成活動ではありません。

why_needed.zh: 输出信息为canonical 事实 OutputCitation-is_a-OutputInformation（OutputCitation is_a OutputInformation）提供明确端点并保持与 OutputCitation 的定义边界。
why_needed.en: output information provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact OutputCitation-is_a-OutputInformation (OutputCitation is_a OutputInformation) while preserving its reviewed boundary from OutputCitation.
why_needed.ja: 出力情報はcanonical 事実 OutputCitation-is_a-OutputInformation（OutputCitation is_a OutputInformation）の明確な端点を提供し、OutputCitation との審査済み境界を保ちながら。

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of OutputInformation: MCP result content and tracing output both require a unified, segmentable, citable, visibility-controlled information surface distinct from execution activity. The ontology-specific boundary for OutputInformation is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Specification > Architecture, base protocol, and server features

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of OutputInformation: MCP result content and tracing output both require a unified, segmentable, citable, visibility-controlled information surface distinct from execution activity. The ontology-specific boundary for OutputInformation is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Tracing guide > Traces, spans, and trace processors

short_definitions.zh: 披露控制产生的、记录可见、抑制或截断内容及理由的信息制品。
short_definitions.en: An information artifact produced by disclosure control that records visible, suppressed, or truncated content and rationale.
short_definitions.ja: 開示制御が生成し、可視・抑制・切詰め内容と理由を記録する情報成果物。

definitions.zh: DisclosureArtifact 是具有身份、产生它的选择或披露活动、接收方、边界、策略依据和被处理内容引用的信息制品；它用于审计披露结果，而不等同于披露策略或活动。
definitions.en: DisclosureArtifact is identity-bearing information with the producing selection or disclosure activity, recipient, boundary, policy basis, and handled-content reference. It audits disclosure outcomes and is not the disclosure policy or activity.
definitions.ja: DisclosureArtifact は識別・生成選択／開示活動・受信者・境界・方針根拠・処理内容参照を持つ情報成果物で、開示結果を監査し方針や活動自体ではありません。

why_needed.zh: 披露审计制品为canonical 事实 DisclosedOutputSegment-is_a-DisclosureArtifact（DisclosedOutputSegment is_a DisclosureArtifact）提供明确端点并保持与 DisclosedOutputSegment 的定义边界。
why_needed.en: disclosure audit artifact provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact DisclosedOutputSegment-is_a-DisclosureArtifact (DisclosedOutputSegment is_a DisclosureArtifact) while preserving its reviewed boundary from DisclosedOutputSegment.
why_needed.ja: 開示監査成果物はcanonical 事実 DisclosedOutputSegment-is_a-DisclosureArtifact（DisclosedOutputSegment is_a DisclosureArtifact）の明確な端点を提供し、DisclosedOutputSegment との審査済み境界を保ちながら。

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of DisclosureArtifact: Observable output and context selection both require records of what was visible or omitted and why; this anchor groups outcome artifacts rather than control policies. The ontology-specific boundary for DisclosureArtifact is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Tracing guide > Traces, spans, and trace processors

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of DisclosureArtifact: Observable output and context selection both require records of what was visible or omitted and why; this anchor groups outcome artifacts rather than control policies. The ontology-specific boundary for DisclosureArtifact is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Paper > COMPASS: Enhancing Agent Long-Horizon Reasoning with Evolving Context; abstract and method or system-architecture sections

short_definitions.zh: 访问路径描述在权限、策略和信任边界控制下可用于取回来源引用的路径或句柄。
short_definitions.en: describes the path or handle through which a source reference can be retrieved, subject to permission, policy, and trust boundary controls.
short_definitions.ja: アクセス経路は、権限、方針、信頼境界の制御下で出典参照を取得するための経路またはハンドルを記述します。

definitions.en: describes the path or handle through which a source reference can be retrieved, subject to permission, policy, and trust boundary controls.
definitions.zh: 访问路径描述在权限、策略和信任边界控制下可用于取回来源引用的路径或句柄。
definitions.ja: アクセス経路は、権限、方針、信頼境界の制御下で出典参照を取得するための経路またはハンドルを記述します。

why_needed.zh: 访问路径为canonical 事实 SourceReference-accessed_via-AccessPath（SourceReference accessed_via AccessPath）提供明确端点并保持与 SourceReference 的定义边界。
why_needed.en: access path provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact SourceReference-accessed_via-AccessPath (SourceReference accessed_via AccessPath) while preserving its reviewed boundary from SourceReference.
why_needed.ja: アクセス経路はcanonical 事実 SourceReference-accessed_via-AccessPath（SourceReference accessed_via AccessPath）の明確な端点を提供し、SourceReference との審査済み境界を保ちながら。

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of AccessPath: AccessPath is information describing how to resolve a source reference under permission and policy constraints, not a subtype of source object or location. The ontology-specific boundary for AccessPath is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Specification > Architecture, base protocol, and server features

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of AccessPath: AccessPath is information describing how to resolve a source reference under permission and policy constraints, not a subtype of source object or location. The ontology-specific boundary for AccessPath is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.

short_definitions.zh: 引用锚点把可见引用绑定到支撑消息、输出、摘要或产物声明的精确来源引用与来源片段。
short_definitions.en: binds a visible citation to the exact source reference and span that support a message, output, summary, or artifact claim.
short_definitions.ja: 引用アンカーは、メッセージ、出力、要約、成果物の主張を支える正確な出典参照と出典断片へ可視引用を結び付けます。

definitions.en: binds a visible citation to the exact source reference and span that support a message, output, summary, or artifact claim.

why_needed.ja: 引用アンカーはcanonical 事実 CitationAnchor-anchors-SourceSpan（CitationAnchor anchors SourceSpan）の明確な端点を提供し、SourceAnchor との審査済み境界を保ちながら。

short_definitions.zh: 内容摘要记录用于去重、完整性检查、缓存验证或引用稳定性的紧凑内容指纹。
short_definitions.en: records a compact fingerprint of content used for deduplication, integrity checks, cache validation, or citation stability.
short_definitions.ja: 内容ダイジェストは、重複排除、完全性確認、キャッシュ検証、引用安定性に用いる小さな内容指紋を記録します。

definitions.en: records a compact fingerprint of content used for deduplication, integrity checks, cache validation, or citation stability.
definitions.zh: 内容摘要记录用于去重、完整性检查、缓存验证或引用稳定性的紧凑内容指纹。
definitions.ja: 内容ダイジェストは、重複排除、完全性確認、キャッシュ検証、引用安定性に用いる小さな内容指紋を記録します。

why_needed.en: content digest provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact ContentDigest-is_a-SourceIdentity (ContentDigest is_a SourceIdentity) while preserving its reviewed boundary from SourceVersion.
why_needed.ja: 内容ダイジェストはcanonical 事実 ContentDigest-is_a-SourceIdentity（ContentDigest is_a SourceIdentity）の明確な端点を提供し、SourceVersion との審査済み境界を保ちながら。

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of ContentDigest: Every ContentDigest is a SourceIdentity computed from source content for equivalence, caching, or integrity checks. The ontology-specific boundary for ContentDigest is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.

short_definitions.zh: 数据库行引用标识可支撑上下文内容的具体数据库行、查询结果或表格切片，同时不拥有数据库本身。
short_definitions.en: references a specific database row, query result, or table slice that can support context content without owning the database.
short_definitions.ja: データベース行参照は、コンテキスト内容を支える具体的な行、問い合わせ結果、表の切片を識別し、データベース自体は所有しません。

definitions.en: references a specific database row, query result, or table slice that can support context content without owning the database.
definitions.zh: 数据库行引用标识可支撑上下文内容的具体数据库行、查询结果或表格切片，同时不拥有数据库本身。
definitions.ja: データベース行参照は、コンテキスト内容を支える具体的な行、問い合わせ結果、表の切片を識別し、データベース自体は所有しません。

short_definitions.en: points from a context artifact to the data zone whose visibility, retention, and handling policy governs that artifact.
short_definitions.ja: データゾーン参照は、コンテキスト成果物を、その可視性、保持期間、取り扱い方針を管理するデータゾーンへ指し示します。

definitions.en: points from a context artifact to the data zone whose visibility, retention, and handling policy governs that artifact.
definitions.zh: 数据区域引用把上下文产物指向管理其可见性、保留期限和处理策略的数据区域。
definitions.ja: データゾーン参照は、コンテキスト成果物を、その可視性、保持期間、取り扱い方針を管理するデータゾーンへ指し示します。

why_needed.en: data zone reference provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact DataZoneReference-refers_to-DataZone (DataZoneReference refers_to DataZone) while preserving its reviewed boundary from DataZone.
why_needed.ja: データ区域参照はcanonical 事実 DataZoneReference-refers_to-DataZone（DataZoneReference refers_to DataZone）の明確な端点を提供し、DataZone との審査済み境界を保ちながら。

short_definitions.zh: 文件资源引用通过路径、统一资源标识符、版本、摘要或片段引用文件类资源，而不建模完整文件系统。
short_definitions.en: references a file-like resource by path, URI, version, digest, or span without modeling the full file system as context input.
short_definitions.ja: ファイル資源参照は、パス、統一資源識別子、バージョン、ダイジェスト、断片によってファイル状資源を参照し、完全なファイルシステムはモデル化しません。

definitions.en: references a file-like resource by path, URI, version, digest, or span without modeling the full file system as context input.

short_definitions.zh: 规定编排中分解、委派、执行或汇报责任的角色。
short_definitions.en: A role specifying decomposition, delegation, performance, or reporting responsibility in orchestration.
short_definitions.ja: 編成における分解、委任、実行、報告責任を規定する役割。

definitions.zh: 协调角色是在编排关系中规定一个参与主体承担何种分解、委派、执行、监督、汇总或汇报责任，并可通过 ActorRoleBinding 赋予主体的角色上位概念。
definitions.en: A CoordinationRole specifies decomposition, delegation, performance, supervision, synthesis, or reporting responsibilities in orchestration and is borne by an actor through an ActorRoleBinding.
definitions.ja: CoordinationRole は編成関係で主体が担う分解、委任、実行、監督、統合、報告責任を規定し、ActorRoleBinding を通じて主体に付与される役割上位概念である。

why_needed.zh: 协调角色为canonical 事实 Orchestrator-is_a-CoordinationRole（Orchestrator is_a CoordinationRole）提供明确端点并保持与 Orchestrator 的定义边界。

short_definitions.zh: 把有边界工作交由另一角色完成并保留责任链的活动。
short_definitions.en: An activity assigning bounded work to another role while preserving accountability lineage.
short_definitions.ja: 境界付き作業を別の役割へ委ね責任系譜を保持する活動。

definitions.zh: 委派过程是委派者依据合同把有范围的工作、权限、上下文与预算交给代理角色执行，并接收可追踪结果而保持或转移明确归属的协调活动。
definitions.en: A DelegationProcess is a coordination activity in which a delegator assigns scoped work, authority, context, and budget under a contract to a delegate role and receives a traceable result with explicit retained or transferred ownership.
definitions.ja: DelegationProcess は委任者が契約に基づき範囲付き作業、権限、文脈、予算を代理役割へ割り当て、明示された保持・移転帰属と共に追跡可能な結果を受け取る調整活動である。

why_needed.zh: 委派过程为canonical 事实 DelegationProcess-governed_by-DelegationContract（DelegationProcess governed_by DelegationContract）提供明确端点并保持与 DelegationContract 的定义边界。
why_needed.en: delegation process provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact DelegationProcess-governed_by-DelegationContract (DelegationProcess governed_by DelegationContract) while preserving its reviewed boundary from DelegationContract.
why_needed.ja: 委任プロセスはcanonical 事実 DelegationProcess-governed_by-DelegationContract（DelegationProcess governed_by DelegationContract）の明確な端点を提供し、DelegationContract との審査済み境界を保ちながら。

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of DelegationProcess: Tool-like delegation and task distribution both assign bounded work to another role with accountability lineage and differ in control retention and assignment form. The ontology-specific boundary for DelegationProcess is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.

short_definitions.zh: 记录编排责任、控制或答案当前归属主体的信息。
short_definitions.en: Information recording current ownership of orchestration responsibility, control, or answer.
short_definitions.ja: 編成責任、制御、回答の現在の帰属主体を記録する情報。

definitions.zh: 协调归属记录是在委派、移交或综合的某一时点，说明哪一主体拥有答案责任、下一步控制权或委派责任，并记录范围、有效期和转移来源的信息记录。
definitions.en: A CoordinationOwnershipRecord identifies, at a time during delegation, handoff, or synthesis, which actor owns answer accountability, next-step control, or delegation responsibility, with scope, validity, and transfer provenance.
definitions.ja: CoordinationOwnershipRecord は委任、移交、統合のある時点で、回答責任、次の制御権、委任責任をどの主体が持つかを範囲、有効期間、移転来歴と共に記録する。

short_definitions.zh: 路由决策是可观察决策事件，应用路由策略选择路由目标，并记录为何选择该目标。
short_definitions.en: observable decision event that applies routing policy to select a routing target and record why that target was chosen.

short_definitions.zh: 停止条件说明编排循环必须停止的情形，例如成功、失败、预算耗尽、安全要求或人工决定已达成。

short_definitions.ja: 停止条件は、成功、失敗、予算枯渇、安全要求、人の判断により、オーケストレーションループが停止しなければならない条件です。

definitions.en: condition under which an orchestration loop must stop because success, failure, budget exhaustion, safety, or human decision has been reached.

short_definitions.zh: 规定控制流可选路径、条件或重试行为的规约。
short_definitions.en: A specification of eligible control-flow paths, conditions, or retry behavior.
short_definitions.ja: 選択可能な制御フロー経路、条件、再試行動作を規定する仕様。

definitions.zh: 路由规约是描述编排控制流可采用的路径、选择策略、适用条件、重试界限或终止条件，并可被路由决策应用的规范性信息。
definitions.en: A RoutingSpecification is normative information describing eligible orchestration paths, selection policy, applicable conditions, retry bounds, or termination conditions that a routing decision can apply.
definitions.ja: RoutingSpecification は編成制御フローの選択可能経路、選択ポリシー、適用条件、再試行境界、終了条件を記述し、経路判断が適用できる規範情報である。

why_needed.zh: 路由规约为canonical 事实 RetryPolicy-is_a-RoutingSpecification（RetryPolicy is_a RoutingSpecification）提供明确端点并保持与 RetryPolicy 的定义边界。
why_needed.en: routing specification provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact RetryPolicy-is_a-RoutingSpecification (RetryPolicy is_a RoutingSpecification) while preserving its reviewed boundary from RetryPolicy.
why_needed.ja: 経路仕様はcanonical 事実 RetryPolicy-is_a-RoutingSpecification（RetryPolicy is_a RoutingSpecification）の明確な端点を提供し、RetryPolicy との審査済み境界を保ちながら。

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of RoutingSpecification: Policies, routes, retry policies, and conditions are all applicable normative information whose instances are not decision occurrences. short_definitions.zh: 可被路由或闸门控制逻辑评价的条件规约。
short_definitions.en: A condition specification evaluable by routing or gate control logic.
short_definitions.ja: 経路・ゲート制御ロジックが評価できる条件仕様。

definitions.zh: 路由条件是一种可依据任务状态、追踪证据、策略、预算、安全或人工决定求值，并据此允许分支、通过闸门或终止流程的路由规约。
definitions.en: A RoutingCondition is a RoutingSpecification evaluable from task state, trace evidence, policy, budget, safety, or human decision to enable a branch, pass a gate, or terminate flow.
definitions.ja: RoutingCondition はタスク状態、トレース証拠、ポリシー、予算、安全、人間判断から評価され、分岐許可、ゲート通過、フロー終了に使われる RoutingSpecification である。

why_needed.zh: 路由条件为canonical 事实 RoutingCondition-is_a-RoutingSpecification（RoutingCondition is_a RoutingSpecification）提供明确端点并保持与 RetryPolicy 的定义边界。
why_needed.en: routing condition provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact RoutingCondition-is_a-RoutingSpecification (RoutingCondition is_a RoutingSpecification) while preserving its reviewed boundary from RetryPolicy.
why_needed.ja: 経路条件はcanonical 事実 RoutingCondition-is_a-RoutingSpecification（RoutingCondition is_a RoutingSpecification）の明確な端点を提供し、RetryPolicy との審査済み境界を保ちながら。

short_definitions.zh: 依据条件允许、阻止、改向或升级工作流的控制能力。
short_definitions.en: A control capability that allows, blocks, redirects, or escalates workflow from conditions.
short_definitions.ja: 条件に基づきワークフローを許可、遮断、経路変更、エスカレーションする制御能力。

definitions.zh: 控制闸门是在编排控制点读取一个或多个条件及证据、产生可审计结果并据此允许、阻止、改向、重试、升级或停止下游工作的控制能力上位概念。
definitions.en: A ControlGate is a capability at an orchestration control point that evaluates conditions and evidence, produces an auditable outcome, and allows, blocks, redirects, retries, escalates, or stops downstream work.
definitions.ja: ControlGate は編成制御点で条件と証拠を評価し、監査可能な結果を生成し、下流作業を許可、遮断、経路変更、再試行、エスカレーション、停止する能力上位概念である。

why_needed.zh: 控制闸门能力为canonical 事实 Gate-is_a-ControlGate（Gate is_a ControlGate）提供明确端点并保持与 Gate 的定义边界。
why_needed.en: control gate provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact Gate-is_a-ControlGate (Gate is_a ControlGate) while preserving its reviewed boundary from Gate.
why_needed.ja: 制御ゲート能力はcanonical 事実 Gate-is_a-ControlGate（Gate is_a ControlGate）の明確な端点を提供し、Gate との審査済み境界を保ちながら。

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of ControlGate: Existing Gate is explicitly a capability controlling workflow after condition evaluation, so it strictly inherits this anchor while conditions and outcomes remain relation endpoints. short_definitions.zh: 目标表示用户或系统希望达成的高层意图，是后续拆解为可操作目标、任务和完成准则的起点。
short_definitions.en: high-level user or system intent that motivates an agent workflow before it is decomposed into operational objectives, tasks, or success criteria.

short_definitions.en: operational and measurable target derived from a goal that can be assigned, checked, and traced through tasks, constraints, and completion criteria.
