short_definitions.zh: 警告事件是从运行、工具、策略、检索、审查或遥测证据中提出警告的可观察事件，执行可以在约束下继续。
short_definitions.en: observable event that raises a warning from runtime, tool, policy, retrieval, review, or telemetry evidence while execution may continue under constraints.
short_definitions.ja: 警告イベントは、実行、ツール、ポリシー、検索、レビュー、テレメトリ証拠から警告を発する観測可能イベントであり、実行は制約下で継続できます。

definitions.en: observable event that raises a warning from runtime, tool, policy, retrieval, review, or telemetry evidence while execution may continue under constraints.
definitions.zh: 警告事件是从运行、工具、策略、检索、审查或遥测证据中提出警告的可观察事件，执行可以在约束下继续。
definitions.ja: 警告イベントは、実行、ツール、ポリシー、検索、レビュー、テレメトリ証拠から警告を発する観測可能イベントであり、実行は制約下で継続できます。

why_needed.zh: 警告事件为canonical 事实 WarningEvent-raises-Warning（WarningEvent raises Warning）提供明确端点并保持与 ErrorEvent 的定义边界。
why_needed.en: warning event provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact WarningEvent-raises-Warning (WarningEvent raises Warning) while preserving its reviewed boundary from ErrorEvent.
why_needed.ja: 警告イベントはcanonical 事実 WarningEvent-raises-Warning（WarningEvent raises Warning）の明確な端点を提供し、ErrorEvent との審査済み境界を保ちながら。

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of WarningEvent: A WarningEvent is a FailureEvent specialization in which execution may continue under warning constraints. The ontology-specific boundary for WarningEvent is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation page > LangSmith docs heading and documented conceptual model

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of WarningEvent: A WarningEvent is a FailureEvent specialization in which execution may continue under warning constraints. The ontology-specific boundary for WarningEvent is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.

short_definitions.zh: 表达警告、风险、置信或诊断说明的非发生性信息。
short_definitions.en: Non-occurrence information expressing warning, risk, confidence, or diagnostic explanation.
short_definitions.ja: 警告・リスク・信頼度・診断説明を表す非発生性情報。

definitions.zh: DiagnosticSignal 是附着于运行、尝试、结果或评审的信息记录，用于表达警告、风险、置信和诊断；它可以由事件产生，但不等同于事件发生。
definitions.en: DiagnosticSignal is information attached to a run, attempt, result, or review to express warning, risk, confidence, or diagnosis; an event may emit it, but it is not the occurrence itself.
definitions.ja: DiagnosticSignal は実行・試行・結果・レビューに付着し警告・リスク・信頼度・診断を表す情報で、事象から発行され得るが発生自体ではない。

why_needed.zh: 诊断信号为canonical 事实 ConfidenceSignal-is_a-DiagnosticSignal（ConfidenceSignal is_a DiagnosticSignal）提供明确端点并保持与 ConfidenceSignal 的定义边界。
why_needed.en: Diagnostic Signal provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact ConfidenceSignal-is_a-DiagnosticSignal (ConfidenceSignal is_a DiagnosticSignal) while preserving its reviewed boundary from ConfidenceSignal.
why_needed.ja: 診断信号はcanonical 事実 ConfidenceSignal-is_a-DiagnosticSignal（ConfidenceSignal is_a DiagnosticSignal）の明確な端点を提供し、ConfidenceSignal との審査済み境界を保ちながら。

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of DiagnosticSignal: All four signal children fully satisfy the definition of diagnostic information. The ontology-specific boundary for DiagnosticSignal is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation page > LangSmith docs heading and documented conceptual model

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of DiagnosticSignal: All four signal children fully satisfy the definition of diagnostic information. The ontology-specific boundary for DiagnosticSignal is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.

short_definitions.zh: 记录错误或受约束警告实际发生的可定位事件。
short_definitions.en: Locatable occurrence recording an error or constrained-warning event.
short_definitions.ja: エラーまたは制約付き警告の実際の発生を記録する特定可能な事象。

definitions.zh: FailureEvent 是在明确运行、调用、协议或策略位置发生的异常、失败或警告事件，具有时间、位置和影响；失败模式分类它，诊断信号解释它。
definitions.en: FailureEvent is an abnormal, failed, or warning occurrence at an explicit run, call, protocol, or policy location, with time, location, and impact; a failure mode classifies it and diagnostic signals explain it.
definitions.ja: FailureEvent は明示的な実行・呼び出し・プロトコル・方針位置で起きる異常・失敗・警告事象で、時刻・場所・影響を持ち、失敗モードが分類し診断信号が説明する。

why_needed.zh: 失败相关事件为canonical 事实 ErrorEvent-is_a-FailureEvent（ErrorEvent is_a FailureEvent）提供明确端点并保持与 ErrorEvent 的定义边界。
why_needed.en: Failure Event provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact ErrorEvent-is_a-FailureEvent (ErrorEvent is_a FailureEvent) while preserving its reviewed boundary from ErrorEvent.
why_needed.ja: 失敗関連事象はcanonical 事実 ErrorEvent-is_a-FailureEvent（ErrorEvent is_a FailureEvent）の明確な端点を提供し、ErrorEvent との審査済み境界を保ちながら。

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of FailureEvent: ErrorEvent and WarningEvent are both actual occurrences with time and location. The ontology-specific boundary for FailureEvent is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation page > LangSmith docs heading and documented conceptual model

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of FailureEvent: ErrorEvent and WarningEvent are both actual occurrences with time and location. The ontology-specific boundary for FailureEvent is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Tracing guide > Traces, spans, and trace processors

short_definitions.zh: 分块是文档、输出、消息或记忆项的有界片段，包含来源片段、边界、来源和检索元数据，用于索引与上下文组装。
short_definitions.en: bounded segment of a document, output, message, or memory item with source span, boundary, provenance, and retrieval metadata used for indexing and context assembly.
short_definitions.ja: チャンクは、文書、出力、メッセージ、記憶項目の境界付き断片で、出典範囲、境界、来歴、検索メタデータを持ち、索引付けとコンテキスト組み立てに使われます。

definitions.en: bounded segment of a document, output, message, or memory item with source span, boundary, provenance, and retrieval metadata used for indexing and context assembly.
definitions.zh: 分块是文档、输出、消息或记忆项的有界片段，包含来源片段、边界、来源和检索元数据，用于索引与上下文组装。
definitions.ja: チャンクは、文書、出力、メッセージ、記憶項目の境界付き断片で、出典範囲、境界、来歴、検索メタデータを持ち、索引付けとコンテキスト組み立てに使われます。

why_needed.zh: 分块为canonical 事实 chunk_derived_from_document（Chunk chunk_derived_from_document Document）提供明确端点并保持与 ChunkReference 的定义边界；节点字段 chunk_id、ordinal 使该区别可验证。
why_needed.en: chunk provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact chunk_derived_from_document (Chunk chunk_derived_from_document Document) while preserving its reviewed boundary from ChunkReference; node fields chunk_id, ordinal make that distinction testable.
why_needed.ja: チャンクはcanonical 事実 chunk_derived_from_document（Chunk chunk_derived_from_document Document）の明確な端点を提供し、ChunkReference との審査済み境界を保ちながら。ノード項目 chunk_id、ordinal によりその区別を検証できます。

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of Chunk: Every Chunk is a ChunkArtifact distinguished by a bounded source span and retrieval metadata. The ontology-specific boundary for Chunk is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation page > LangChain agents heading and documented conceptual model

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of Chunk: Every Chunk is a ChunkArtifact distinguished by a bounded source span and retrieval metadata. The ontology-specific boundary for Chunk is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Paper > COMPASS: Enhancing Agent Long-Horizon Reasoning with Evolving Context; abstract and method or system-architecture sections

short_definitions.zh: 分块边界是分块的结构边界，例如字节偏移、令牌跨度、行范围、页范围、章节断点、语义边界或重叠边界。
short_definitions.en: structural boundary for a chunk, such as byte offset, token span, line range, page range, section break, semantic boundary, or overlap boundary.
short_definitions.ja: チャンク境界は、バイトオフセット、トークン範囲、行範囲、ページ範囲、節の切れ目、意味境界、重なり境界など、チャンクの構造境界です。

definitions.en: structural boundary for a chunk, such as byte offset, token span, line range, page range, section break, semantic boundary, or overlap boundary.
definitions.zh: 分块边界是分块的结构边界，例如字节偏移、令牌跨度、行范围、页范围、章节断点、语义边界或重叠边界。
definitions.ja: チャンク境界は、バイトオフセット、トークン範囲、行範囲、ページ範囲、節の切れ目、意味境界、重なり境界など、チャンクの構造境界です。

why_needed.en: chunk boundary provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact chunk_has_boundary (Chunk chunk_has_boundary ChunkBoundary) while preserving its reviewed boundary from ChunkContextNote.
why_needed.ja: チャンク境界はcanonical 事実 chunk_has_boundary（Chunk chunk_has_boundary ChunkBoundary）の明確な端点を提供し、ChunkContextNote との審査済み境界を保ちながら。

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of ChunkBoundary: A ChunkBoundary is ChunkMetadata specifying offsets, page or line ranges, or semantic breaks. The ontology-specific boundary for ChunkBoundary is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation page > Deep Agents JS docs heading and documented conceptual model

short_definitions.zh: 旧定义的“压缩产物”会混淆输入与过程；裁决为保留来源锚点并产生新 Chunk 的活动，而产物仍是 Chunk。
short_definitions.en: The legacy 'compression artifact' wording conflates process and output; this is reviewed as an activity that preserves source anchors and produces a new Chunk, whose output remains a Chunk.
short_definitions.ja: 旧定義の「圧縮成果物」は工程と出力を混同するため、出典アンカーを保ち新 Chunk を生成する活動へ再分類し、成果物は Chunk のままとします。

short_definitions.en: short note attached to a chunk that explains its local section, surrounding context, or reason for retrieval relevance.

short_definitions.zh: 分块运行是把文档、输出或记忆项切分为可检索分块的可观测事件，并记录边界、重叠、片段和来源元数据。

short_definitions.en: overlap span shared between adjacent chunks to preserve local continuity during retrieval and context assembly.
short_definitions.ja: チャンク重複は、検索とコンテキスト組み立てで局所的な連続性を保つために隣接チャンクが共有する重なり範囲です。

labels.ja: 位置付けプロンプト実行

short_definitions.zh: 定位提示运行是为分块补充文档局部或任务局部上下文的可观测事件，使检索保留章节含义、来源目的和周边证据。
short_definitions.en: observable event that adds document-local or task-local context to a chunk so retrieval can preserve section meaning, source purpose, and surrounding evidence.
short_definitions.ja: 位置付けプロンプト実行は、検索が節の意味、出典の目的、周辺証拠を保てるように、チャンクへ文書内またはタスク内の文脈を加える観測可能なイベントです。

definitions.en: observable event that adds document-local or task-local context to a chunk so retrieval can preserve section meaning, source purpose, and surrounding evidence.
definitions.zh: 定位提示运行是为分块补充文档局部或任务局部上下文的可观测事件，使检索保留章节含义、来源目的和周边证据。
definitions.ja: 位置付けプロンプト実行は、検索が節の意味、出典の目的、周辺証拠を保てるように、チャンクへ文書内またはタスク内の文脈を加える観測可能なイベントです。

why_needed.zh: 分块定位提示运行为canonical 事实 SituatingPromptRun-consumes-Chunk（SituatingPromptRun consumes Chunk）提供明确端点并保持与 ChunkCompression 的定义边界。
why_needed.en: situating prompt run provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact SituatingPromptRun-consumes-Chunk (SituatingPromptRun consumes Chunk) while preserving its reviewed boundary from ChunkCompression.
why_needed.ja: 位置付けプロンプト実行はcanonical 事実 SituatingPromptRun-consumes-Chunk（SituatingPromptRun consumes Chunk）の明確な端点を提供し、ChunkCompression との審査済み境界を保ちながら。

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of SituatingPromptRun: A SituatingPromptRun is a ChunkProcessingActivity that adds traceable local grounding to a Chunk. The ontology-specific boundary for SituatingPromptRun is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation page > Deep Agents JS docs heading and documented conceptual model

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of SituatingPromptRun: A SituatingPromptRun is a ChunkProcessingActivity that adds traceable local grounding to a Chunk. The ontology-specific boundary for SituatingPromptRun is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Paper > COMPASS: Enhancing Agent Long-Horizon Reasoning with Evolving Context; abstract and method or system-architecture sections

short_definitions.zh: 在分块处理与检索之间保存有界内容或其精确引用的信息制品。
short_definitions.en: An information artifact preserving bounded content or its exact reference between chunk processing and retrieval.
short_definitions.ja: チャンク処理と検索の間で有界内容または正確な参照を保持する情報成果物です。

definitions.zh: ChunkArtifact 是具有来源身份、版本和跨度的信息制品，用于承载一个 Chunk、增加局部语境的 SituatedChunk，或对确切 Chunk/来源跨度的 ChunkReference；它不包含分块运行、边界参数或纯质量判断。
definitions.en: A ChunkArtifact is a provenance-, version-, and span-bearing information artifact that carries a Chunk, a locally grounded SituatedChunk, or a ChunkReference to an exact chunk and source span. It excludes chunking runs, boundary parameters, and standalone quality judgments.
definitions.ja: ChunkArtifact は来歴・版・範囲を持ち、Chunk、局所根拠付き SituatedChunk、正確なチャンク／出典範囲への ChunkReference を担う情報成果物です。分割実行・境界パラメータ・単独品質判断を含みません。

why_needed.zh: 分块信息制品为canonical 事实 Chunk-is_a-ChunkArtifact（Chunk is_a ChunkArtifact）提供明确端点并保持与 Chunk 的定义边界。
why_needed.en: chunk artifact provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact Chunk-is_a-ChunkArtifact (Chunk is_a ChunkArtifact) while preserving its reviewed boundary from Chunk.
why_needed.ja: チャンク情報成果物はcanonical 事実 Chunk-is_a-ChunkArtifact（Chunk is_a ChunkArtifact）の明確な端点を提供し、Chunk との審査済み境界を保ちながら。

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of ChunkArtifact: LlamaIndex node/chunk retrieval contracts and Agentic Memory's retrievable memory units jointly support a provenance-bearing chunk information root. The ontology-specific boundary for ChunkArtifact is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation page > LlamaIndex agents docs heading and documented conceptual model

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of ChunkArtifact: LlamaIndex node/chunk retrieval contracts and Agentic Memory's retrievable memory units jointly support a provenance-bearing chunk information root. The ontology-specific boundary for ChunkArtifact is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Paper > Agentic Memory; abstract and method or system-architecture sections

short_definitions.zh: 描述分块边界、重叠、来源、质量或局部语境且不承载分块正文的信息。
short_definitions.en: Information describing chunk boundaries, overlap, provenance, quality, or local context without carrying the chunk body.
short_definitions.ja: チャンク本文を担わず境界・重複・来歴・品質・局所文脈を記述する情報です。

definitions.zh: ChunkMetadata 是附着于 Chunk 或 ChunkingRun 的可审计信息，具有明确元数据种类、值或证据与来源，包括 ChunkBoundary、ChunkOverlap、ChunkProvenance、ChunkQualitySignal 和 ChunkContextNote；它不是 Chunk 本身，也不是改变 Chunk 的活动。
definitions.en: ChunkMetadata is auditable information attached to a Chunk or ChunkingRun with an explicit metadata kind, value or evidence, and provenance. It includes ChunkBoundary, ChunkOverlap, ChunkProvenance, ChunkQualitySignal, and ChunkContextNote. It is neither the Chunk itself nor an activity changing it.
definitions.ja: ChunkMetadata は Chunk／ChunkingRun に付く監査可能情報で、明示的種類・値／証拠・来歴を持ち、ChunkBoundary・ChunkOverlap・ChunkProvenance・ChunkQualitySignal・ChunkContextNote を含みます。Chunk 自体でも変更活動でもありません。

why_needed.zh: 分块元数据为canonical 事实 Chunk-has_metadata-ChunkMetadata（Chunk has_metadata ChunkMetadata）提供明确端点并保持与 Chunk 的定义边界。
why_needed.en: chunk metadata provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact Chunk-has_metadata-ChunkMetadata (Chunk has_metadata ChunkMetadata) while preserving its reviewed boundary from Chunk.
why_needed.ja: チャンクメタデータはcanonical 事実 Chunk-has_metadata-ChunkMetadata（Chunk has_metadata ChunkMetadata）の明確な端点を提供し、Chunk との審査済み境界を保ちながら。

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of ChunkMetadata: LlamaIndex chunk metadata practice and MemoryAgentBench's evaluation of provenance-aware memory independently support this information category. The ontology-specific boundary for ChunkMetadata is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation page > LlamaIndex agents docs heading and documented conceptual model

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of ChunkMetadata: LlamaIndex chunk metadata practice and MemoryAgentBench's evaluation of provenance-aware memory independently support this information category. The ontology-specific boundary for ChunkMetadata is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: README > MemoryAgentBench overview, architecture, and documented interfaces

short_definitions.zh: 消费文档或分块并产生新分块表示且保留来源的可观测活动。
short_definitions.en: An observable activity consuming a document or chunk and producing a new chunk representation while preserving provenance.
short_definitions.ja: 文書・チャンクを消費し来歴を保つ新チャンク表現を生成する観測可能活動です。

definitions.zh: ChunkProcessingActivity 是带运行身份、输入版本、策略或模型、输出版本、时间、结果和错误的活动，覆盖 ChunkingRun、SituatingPromptRun 和 ChunkCompression；产物通过 produces 指向 Chunk/SituatedChunk，本活动本身不作为信息制品。
definitions.en: A ChunkProcessingActivity is an activity with run identity, input version, strategy or model, output version, time, result, and error. It covers ChunkingRun, SituatingPromptRun, and ChunkCompression. Its products point to Chunk or SituatedChunk through produces; the activity itself is not an information artifact.
definitions.ja: ChunkProcessingActivity は実行識別・入力版・方策／モデル・出力版・時刻・結果・エラーを持ち、ChunkingRun・SituatingPromptRun・ChunkCompression を含みます。成果物は produces で Chunk／SituatedChunk を指し、活動自体は情報成果物ではありません。

why_needed.zh: 分块处理活动为canonical 事实 ChunkCompression-is_a-ChunkProcessingActivity（ChunkCompression is_a ChunkProcessingActivity）提供明确端点并保持与 ChunkCompression 的定义边界。
why_needed.en: chunk processing activity provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact ChunkCompression-is_a-ChunkProcessingActivity (ChunkCompression is_a ChunkProcessingActivity) while preserving its reviewed boundary from ChunkCompression.
why_needed.ja: チャンク処理活動はcanonical 事実 ChunkCompression-is_a-ChunkProcessingActivity（ChunkCompression is_a ChunkProcessingActivity）の明確な端点を提供し、ChunkCompression との審査済み境界を保ちながら。

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of ChunkProcessingActivity: LlamaIndex workflows model chunking and transformation as input/output steps, while Agentic Memory treats memory construction as auditable operations; together they support this activity anchor. The ontology-specific boundary for ChunkProcessingActivity is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation page > LlamaIndex workflows heading and documented conceptual model

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of ChunkProcessingActivity: LlamaIndex workflows model chunking and transformation as input/output steps, while Agentic Memory treats memory construction as auditable operations; together they support this activity anchor. The ontology-specific boundary for ChunkProcessingActivity is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Paper > Agentic Memory; abstract and method or system-architecture sections

short_definitions.zh: 上下文组装是选择、排序、预算和排除信息的可观测活动，通过关系产生窗口与 ContextPackage。
short_definitions.en: ContextAssembly is an observable activity selecting, ordering, budgeting, and excluding information and producing a window and ContextPackage by relation.
short_definitions.ja: ContextAssembly は情報を選択・順序付け・予算配分・除外し、関係で窓と ContextPackage を生成する観測可能活動です。

definitions.zh: 上下文组装是选择、排序、预算和排除信息的可观测活动，通过关系产生窗口与 ContextPackage。

short_definitions.zh: 上下文预算定义决定哪些信息可以进入运行上下文时使用的令牌、窗口、成本、新鲜度和优先级限制。

labels.ja: コンテキスト除外判断

short_definitions.zh: 它记录一次材料因隐私、信任、相关性、安全或预算被排除的决定，不是可复用规则的子类。
short_definitions.en: It records one decision to omit material for privacy, trust, relevance, safety, or budget and is not a subtype of a reusable rule.

labels.ja: コンテキスト順序規則

short_definitions.zh: 上下文排序规则按优先级、近因性、新鲜度、权威、来源信任、检索排名、任务相关性、消息角色和预算压力排序上下文。

labels.ja: コンテキスト更新イベント

short_definitions.zh: 上下文刷新事件是在新证据、反馈或状态变化后重新运行检索、替换陈旧记忆、重算摘要或重排上下文的可观测更新。
short_definitions.en: observable update that reruns retrieval, replaces stale memory, recomputes summaries, or reorders context after new evidence, feedback, or state changes.
short_definitions.ja: コンテキスト更新イベントは、新しい証拠、フィードバック、状態変化の後に検索を再実行し、古い記憶を置き換え、要約を再計算し、文脈を並べ替える観測可能な更新です。

definitions.en: observable update that reruns retrieval, replaces stale memory, recomputes summaries, or reorders context after new evidence, feedback, or state changes.
definitions.zh: 上下文刷新事件是在新证据、反馈或状态变化后重新运行检索、替换陈旧记忆、重算摘要或重排上下文的可观测更新。
definitions.ja: コンテキスト更新イベントは、新しい証拠、フィードバック、状態変化の後に検索を再実行し、古い記憶を置き換え、要約を再計算し、文脈を並べ替える観測可能な更新です。

short_definitions.zh: 为一次模型调用或 Agent 步骤承载、组织或解释可见上下文的信息制品。
short_definitions.en: An information artifact carrying, organizing, or explaining visible context for one model call or agent step.
short_definitions.ja: 一回のモデル呼出し・Agent ステップ用の可視文脈を保持・組織・説明する情報成果物です。

definitions.zh: ContextArtifact 是具有上下文版本、来源、预算位置与消费者关联的信息制品，覆盖 ContextWindow、ContextSummary、ContextSlot 和 ContextSource；最终 ContextPackage 由 Info 域唯一拥有并通过关系引用，ContextAssembly/Refresh 是活动或事件，规则不属于本类。
definitions.en: A ContextArtifact is information with context version, provenance, budget placement, and consumer association. It covers ContextWindow, ContextSummary, ContextSlot, and ContextSource. Final ContextPackage is uniquely owned by Info and referenced by relation; ContextAssembly and refresh are activities or events, and rules are excluded.
definitions.ja: ContextArtifact は文脈版・来歴・予算位置・消費者関連を持ち、ContextWindow・ContextSummary・ContextSlot・ContextSource を含みます。最終 ContextPackage は Info が一意所有し関係参照し、ContextAssembly／更新は活動／イベント、規則は除外します。

why_needed.zh: 上下文信息制品为canonical 事实 ContextSlot-is_a-ContextArtifact（ContextSlot is_a ContextArtifact）提供明确端点并保持与 ContextSlot 的定义边界。
why_needed.en: context artifact provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact ContextSlot-is_a-ContextArtifact (ContextSlot is_a ContextArtifact) while preserving its reviewed boundary from ContextSlot.

short_definitions.zh: 规定上下文信息排序、预算或纳入条件的版本化规范。
short_definitions.en: A versioned specification governing ordering, budgeting, or inclusion conditions for context information.
short_definitions.ja: 文脈情報の順序・予算・採用条件を統治する版付き仕様です。

definitions.zh: ContextRule 是具有身份、版本、适用范围、优先级和评估条件的规范，覆盖 ContextOrderingRule 与 ContextBudget；一次 ContextExclusion 是应用规则后的决定，通过关系记录，不是规则子类。
definitions.en: A ContextRule is a specification with identity, version, applicability scope, priority, and evaluation conditions. It covers ContextOrderingRule and ContextBudget. A ContextExclusion is a decision resulting from applying rules and is recorded by relation rather than subclassing the rule.
definitions.ja: ContextRule は識別・版・適用範囲・優先度・評価条件を持つ仕様で ContextOrderingRule・ContextBudget を含みます。一回の ContextExclusion は規則適用後の判断で、規則下位型でなく関係記録します。

definitions.en: retrieval index that combines dense semantic vectors, sparse lexical features, or graph/context signals before scoring or rank fusion.

why_needed.zh: 混合索引为canonical 事实 HybridIndex-is_a-Index（HybridIndex is_a Index）提供明确端点并保持与 TfIdfIndex 的定义边界。

why_needed.ja: ハイブリッド索引はcanonical 事実 HybridIndex-is_a-Index（HybridIndex is_a Index）の明確な端点を提供し、TfIdfIndex との審査済み境界を保ちながら。

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of HybridIndex: A HybridIndex is an Index combining dense, sparse, lexical, or graph signals. The ontology-specific boundary for HybridIndex is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation page > LangChain agents heading and documented conceptual model

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of HybridIndex: A HybridIndex is an Index combining dense, sparse, lexical, or graph signals. The ontology-specific boundary for HybridIndex is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.

short_definitions.zh: 索引构建运行是从记忆记录、分块、嵌入、词汇特征或图链接构建或刷新索引并产生索引版本的可观测事件。

definitions.en: observable event that builds or refreshes an index from memory records, chunks, embeddings, lexical features, or graph links and emits an IndexVersion.

definitions.en: sparse vector representation such as weighted token, term, or feature dimensions used for lexical or hybrid retrieval over memory records and chunks.

definitions.en: index structure that organizes embedding vectors and metadata so retrieval queries can return similar memory records, chunks, or context candidates.
definitions.zh: 向量索引组织嵌入向量和元数据，使检索查询能够返回相似的记忆记录、分块或上下文候选。
definitions.ja: ベクトル索引は、検索クエリが類似する記憶レコード、チャンク、文脈候補を返せるように、埋め込みベクトルとメタデータを組織する索引構造です。

why_needed.zh: 向量索引为canonical 事实 vector_index_contains_embedding（VectorIndex vector_index_contains_embedding EmbeddingVector）提供明确端点并保持与 HybridIndex 的定义边界。
why_needed.en: vector index provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact vector_index_contains_embedding (VectorIndex vector_index_contains_embedding EmbeddingVector) while preserving its reviewed boundary from HybridIndex.
why_needed.ja: ベクトル索引はcanonical 事実 vector_index_contains_embedding（VectorIndex vector_index_contains_embedding EmbeddingVector）の明確な端点を提供し、HybridIndex との審査済み境界を保ちながら。

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of VectorIndex: A VectorIndex is an Index organizing vector representations and metadata for nearest-neighbor retrieval. The ontology-specific boundary for VectorIndex is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation page > LangChain agents heading and documented conceptual model

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of VectorIndex: A VectorIndex is an Index organizing vector representations and metadata for nearest-neighbor retrieval. The ontology-specific boundary for VectorIndex is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Paper > COMPASS: Enhancing Agent Long-Horizon Reasoning with Evolving Context; abstract and method or system-architecture sections

short_definitions.zh: 以可比较或可检索形式编码某个来源对象且保留其指向的信息。
short_definitions.en: Information encoding a source object in a comparable or retrievable form while retaining reference to it.
short_definitions.ja: 出典対象への参照を保ち、比較・検索可能形式へ符号化した情報です。

definitions.zh: Representation 是具有表示方法、生成来源、版本和被表示对象的信息，可为 Embedding 或其他向量/结构表示；它不同于生成表示的活动、承载表示的索引以及被表示的 Chunk/MemoryRecord。
definitions.en: A Representation is information with a representation method, generation provenance, version, and represented object. It may be an Embedding or another vector or structured representation. It is distinct from the activity generating it, the index hosting it, and the Chunk or MemoryRecord it represents.
definitions.ja: Representation は表現方式・生成来歴・版・表現対象を持つ情報で、Embedding や他のベクトル／構造表現になれます。生成活動・保持索引・表現対象 Chunk／MemoryRecord とは別です。

why_needed.zh: 表示为canonical 事实 IndexBuildRun-consumes-Representation（IndexBuildRun consumes Representation）提供明确端点并保持与 IndexBuildRun 的定义边界。
why_needed.en: representation provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact IndexBuildRun-consumes-Representation (IndexBuildRun consumes Representation) while preserving its reviewed boundary from IndexBuildRun.
why_needed.ja: 表現はcanonical 事実 IndexBuildRun-consumes-Representation（IndexBuildRun consumes Representation）の明確な端点を提供し、IndexBuildRun との審査済み境界を保ちながら。

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of Representation: LlamaIndex vector/node representation contracts and MAGMA's structured memory representations independently support treating representations as traceable information objects. The ontology-specific boundary for Representation is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation page > LlamaIndex agents docs heading and documented conceptual model

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of Representation: LlamaIndex vector/node representation contracts and MAGMA's structured memory representations independently support treating representations as traceable information objects. The ontology-specific boundary for Representation is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Paper > MAGMA; abstract and method or system-architecture sections

short_definitions.zh: 以有序数值或加权特征维度编码来源对象的 Representation。
short_definitions.en: A Representation encoding a source object as ordered numeric or weighted feature dimensions.
short_definitions.ja: 出典対象を順序付き数値・重み特徴次元で符号化する Representation です。

definitions.zh: VectorRepresentation 是以向量维度、数值类型、稀疏性、距离兼容性和生成版本为区分特征的 Representation，覆盖 DenseVector、SparseVector 和 EmbeddingVector；它不等同于 VectorIndex 或 VectorDatabase。
definitions.en: A VectorRepresentation is a Representation distinguished by vector dimensions, numeric type, sparsity, distance compatibility, and generation version. It covers DenseVector, SparseVector, and EmbeddingVector and is not a VectorIndex or VectorDatabase.
definitions.ja: VectorRepresentation は次元・数値型・疎密・距離互換性・生成版で区別される Representation で DenseVector・SparseVector・EmbeddingVector を含み、VectorIndex／VectorDatabase ではありません。

why_needed.zh: 向量表示为canonical 事实 VectorRepresentation-is_a-Representation（VectorRepresentation is_a Representation）提供明确端点并保持与 Embedding 的定义边界。
why_needed.en: vector representation provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact VectorRepresentation-is_a-Representation (VectorRepresentation is_a Representation) while preserving its reviewed boundary from Embedding.
why_needed.ja: ベクトル表現はcanonical 事実 VectorRepresentation-is_a-Representation（VectorRepresentation is_a Representation）の明確な端点を提供し、Embedding との審査済み境界を保ちながら。

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of VectorRepresentation: The two sources independently use dense/sparse representations and memory-retrieval vectors, supporting a vector parent specialized from Representation. The ontology-specific boundary for VectorRepresentation is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation page > LlamaIndex agents docs heading and documented conceptual model

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of VectorRepresentation: The two sources independently use dense/sparse representations and memory-retrieval vectors, supporting a vector parent specialized from Representation. The ontology-specific boundary for VectorRepresentation is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Paper > Agentic Memory; abstract and method or system-architecture sections

short_definitions.zh: 按一种可声明的组织与查找方法把来源条目映射为可检索候选的索引实体。
short_definitions.en: An index entity mapping source entries to retrievable candidates under a declared organization and lookup method.
short_definitions.ja: 明示した組織・検索方式で出典項目を検索候補へ写像する索引実体です。

definitions.zh: Index 是具有索引种类、配置身份、输入表示要求与版本序列的检索结构，覆盖 VectorIndex、HybridIndex 和 TfIdfIndex；每次构建结果由 IndexVersion 表示，分片和条目通过 part_of 连接，后端数据库通过 hosts 连接。
definitions.en: An Index is a retrieval structure with index kind, configuration identity, input-representation requirements, and a version sequence. It covers VectorIndex, HybridIndex, and TfIdfIndex. Each build result is represented by IndexVersion, shards and entries connect through part_of, and backend databases connect through hosts.
definitions.ja: Index は索引種・構成識別・入力表現要件・版系列を持つ検索構造で VectorIndex・HybridIndex・TfIdfIndex を含みます。各構築結果は IndexVersion、シャード・項目は part_of、バックエンド DB は hosts で接続します。

why_needed.zh: 检索索引为canonical 事实 IndexVersion-version_of-Index（IndexVersion version_of Index）提供明确端点并保持与 IndexVersion 的定义边界。
why_needed.en: index provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact IndexVersion-version_of-Index (IndexVersion version_of Index) while preserving its reviewed boundary from IndexVersion.
why_needed.ja: 検索索引はcanonical 事実 IndexVersion-version_of-Index（IndexVersion version_of Index）の明確な端点を提供し、IndexVersion との審査済み境界を保ちながら。

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of Index: Official LlamaIndex and Haystack retrieval documentation both distinguish index configuration, documents or entries, and build/query execution, supporting this structural parent. The ontology-specific boundary for Index is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation page > Haystack agents docs heading and documented conceptual model

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of Index: Official LlamaIndex and Haystack retrieval documentation both distinguish index configuration, documents or entries, and build/query execution, supporting this structural parent. The ontology-specific boundary for Index is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation page > LlamaIndex agents docs heading and documented conceptual model

short_definitions.zh: 生成表示或构建索引版本且记录输入、配置、时间与结果的可观测活动。
short_definitions.en: An observable activity generating representations or building index versions with recorded inputs, configuration, time, and result.
short_definitions.ja: 入力・構成・時刻・結果を記録し表現生成・索引版構築を行う観測可能活動です。

definitions.zh: IndexActivity 是具有运行身份、输入来源、模型或索引配置、输出版本、结果和错误的活动，覆盖 EmbeddingRun 与 IndexBuildRun；IndexRefreshEvent 保持独立事件并通过 supersedes 连接版本。
definitions.en: An IndexActivity is an activity with run identity, source input, model or index configuration, output version, result, and error. It covers EmbeddingRun and IndexBuildRun. IndexRefreshEvent remains an independent event connected to versions through supersedes.
definitions.ja: IndexActivity は実行識別・出典入力・モデル／索引構成・出力版・結果・エラーを持ち、EmbeddingRun・IndexBuildRun を含みます。IndexRefreshEvent は独立イベントで supersedes により版へ接続します。

why_needed.zh: 索引处理活动为canonical 事实 EmbeddingRun-is_a-IndexActivity（EmbeddingRun is_a IndexActivity）提供明确端点并保持与 EmbeddingRun 的定义边界。
why_needed.en: index activity provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact EmbeddingRun-is_a-IndexActivity (EmbeddingRun is_a IndexActivity) while preserving its reviewed boundary from EmbeddingRun.
why_needed.ja: 索引処理活動はcanonical 事実 EmbeddingRun-is_a-IndexActivity（EmbeddingRun is_a IndexActivity）の明確な端点を提供し、EmbeddingRun との審査済み境界を保ちながら。

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of IndexActivity: LlamaIndex workflows and Haystack pipelines both execute embedding and indexing as explicit input/output steps with failure state, supporting this activity anchor. short_definitions.en: represents a database row or query-result row that may be addressed through a source reference, subject to policy and provenance.

short_definitions.en: An addressable resource that memory ingestion can read under policy while preserving provenance.
short_definitions.ja: 記憶取込が方針下でアドレス指定・読取し来歴を保持できる資源です。

definitions.zh: IngestibleResource 是具有稳定身份或定位信息、访问与信任上下文的实体或信息资源，可被 IngestionRun 通过 DocumentLoader 读取并转换为文档、元数据、分块或记忆记录；它只描述摄入输入，不包括工具能力资源、运行活动或摄入产生的审计事件。
definitions.en: An IngestibleResource is an entity or information resource with stable identity or location plus access and trust context. An IngestionRun may read it through a DocumentLoader and transform it into a document, metadata, chunks, or memory records. It describes ingestion input only and excludes tool capability resources, runtime activities, and ingestion audit events.
definitions.ja: IngestibleResource は安定識別または位置とアクセス・信頼文脈を持つ実体／情報資源で、IngestionRun が DocumentLoader により読み、文書・メタデータ・チャンク・記憶レコードへ変換できます。取込入力だけを表し、ツール能力資源・実行活動・取込監査イベントを除外します。

why_needed.zh: 可摄入资源为canonical 事实 IngestionRun-reads-IngestibleResource（IngestionRun reads IngestibleResource）提供明确端点并保持与 IngestionRun 的定义边界。
why_needed.en: ingestible resource provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact IngestionRun-reads-IngestibleResource (IngestionRun reads IngestibleResource) while preserving its reviewed boundary from IngestionRun.
why_needed.ja: 取込可能資源はcanonical 事実 IngestionRun-reads-IngestibleResource（IngestionRun reads IngestibleResource）の明確な端点を提供し、IngestionRun との審査済み境界を保ちながら。

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of IngestibleResource: Official LlamaIndex and Haystack documentation independently model multi-source loading, documentization, indexing, and retrieval inputs as addressable resources, supporting this ingestion boundary. The ontology-specific boundary for IngestibleResource is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation page > Haystack agents docs heading and documented conceptual model

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of IngestibleResource: Official LlamaIndex and Haystack documentation independently model multi-source loading, documentization, indexing, and retrieval inputs as addressable resources, supporting this ingestion boundary. short_definitions.zh: 记忆审计事件记录谁读取、写入、更新、验证、删除、导出或复用了某条记忆记录，是可观测审计事件。
short_definitions.en: observable audit event that records who read, wrote, updated, validated, deleted, exported, or reused a memory record.
short_definitions.ja: 記憶監査イベントは、誰が記憶レコードを読み取り、書き込み、更新、検証、削除、エクスポート、再利用したかを記録する観測可能な監査イベントです。

definitions.en: observable audit event that records who read, wrote, updated, validated, deleted, exported, or reused a memory record.

short_definitions.zh: 记忆验证是在复用前检查记忆记录的来源、安全、信任边界、新鲜度、冲突、投毒或用户批准的审查或策略操作。

short_definitions.zh: 记忆写入是从消息、工具结果、轨迹事件、经验、偏好、摘要或反思提出或提交新记忆记录的可观测操作。
short_definitions.en: observable operation that proposes or commits a new memory record from a message, tool result, trace event, experience, preference, summary, or reflection.

short_definitions.zh: 对记忆候选或记录执行写入、变更、验证、保留或移除并产生可审计结果的活动。
short_definitions.en: An activity writing, changing, validating, retaining, or removing a memory candidate or record and producing an auditable result.
short_definitions.ja: 記憶候補・記録に書込・変更・検証・保持・除去を行い監査可能結果を生成する活動です。

definitions.zh: MemoryOperation 是具有操作身份、操作者、理由、适用策略、输入版本、输出版本、时间、结果和错误的活动，覆盖写入、更新、合并、删除、丢弃、驱逐、过期、取代、压缩、巩固、验证、反思和衰减；所有变更产生新版本或明确不可用状态，不原地改写旧证据。
definitions.en: A MemoryOperation is an activity with operation identity, actor, reason, applicable policy, input version, output version, time, result, and error. It covers write, update, merge, delete, discard, eviction, expiration, supersession, compaction, consolidation, validation, reflection, and decay. Every change creates a new version or explicit unavailable state and never rewrites old evidence in place.
definitions.ja: MemoryOperation は操作識別・主体・理由・適用方針・入力版・出力版・時刻・結果・エラーを持ち、書込・更新・統合・削除・破棄・退避・期限切れ・置換・圧縮・固定化・検証・省察・減衰を含みます。変更は新版本または明示利用不能状態を作り、旧証拠を直接書換えません。

why_needed.zh: 记忆操作为canonical 事实 MemoryOperation-acts_on-MemoryRecord（MemoryOperation acts_on MemoryRecord）提供明确端点并保持与 MemoryRecord 的定义边界；节点字段 operation_id、occurred_at、reason 使该区别可验证。
why_needed.en: memory operation provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact MemoryOperation-acts_on-MemoryRecord (MemoryOperation acts_on MemoryRecord) while preserving its reviewed boundary from MemoryRecord; node fields operation_id, occurred_at, reason make that distinction testable.
why_needed.ja: 記憶操作はcanonical 事実 MemoryOperation-acts_on-MemoryRecord（MemoryOperation acts_on MemoryRecord）の明確な端点を提供し、MemoryRecord との審査済み境界を保ちながら。ノード項目 operation_id、occurred_at、reason によりその区別を検証できます。

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of MemoryOperation: Memory as Action explicitly treats memory management as agent action, while Agentic Memory provides an evolving memory-operation loop; the independent sources support a common activity contract. The ontology-specific boundary for MemoryOperation is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Paper > Agentic Memory; abstract and method or system-architecture sections

short_definitions.zh: 在一次可重放检索中承载查询、候选、选择结果或轨迹的信息制品。
short_definitions.en: An information artifact carrying a query, candidates, selected result, or trace within a replayable retrieval.
short_definitions.ja: 再生可能検索で問い合わせ・候補・選択結果・トレースを担う情報成果物です。

definitions.zh: RetrievalArtifact 是具有查询或运行关联、来源、索引版本与选择状态的信息制品，覆盖 RetrievalQuery、CandidateSet、CandidateChunk、RetrievedChunk 和 RetrievalTrace；评分由 RetrievalScore 表示，过滤由规范表示，RankFusion/TopKSelection 是活动。
definitions.en: A RetrievalArtifact is an information artifact with query or run association, provenance, index version, and selection state. It covers RetrievalQuery, CandidateSet, CandidateChunk, RetrievedChunk, and RetrievalTrace. Scores are represented by RetrievalScore, filters by specifications, and RankFusion or TopKSelection by activities.
definitions.ja: RetrievalArtifact は問い合わせ／実行関連・来歴・索引版・選択状態を持ち、RetrievalQuery・CandidateSet・CandidateChunk・RetrievedChunk・RetrievalTrace を含みます。スコアは RetrievalScore、フィルタは仕様、RankFusion／TopKSelection は活動です。

why_needed.zh: 检索信息制品为canonical 事实 CandidateChunk-is_a-RetrievalArtifact（CandidateChunk is_a RetrievalArtifact）提供明确端点并保持与 CandidateChunk 的定义边界。
why_needed.en: retrieval artifact provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact CandidateChunk-is_a-RetrievalArtifact (CandidateChunk is_a RetrievalArtifact) while preserving its reviewed boundary from CandidateChunk.
why_needed.ja: 検索情報成果物はcanonical 事実 CandidateChunk-is_a-RetrievalArtifact（CandidateChunk is_a RetrievalArtifact）の明確な端点を提供し、CandidateChunk との審査済み境界を保ちながら。

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of RetrievalArtifact: Agentic Memory retrieval operations and MemoryAgentBench query-candidate-result evaluations both require replayable information boundaries, supporting this anchor. The ontology-specific boundary for RetrievalArtifact is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Paper > Agentic Memory; abstract and method or system-architecture sections

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of RetrievalArtifact: Agentic Memory retrieval operations and MemoryAgentBench query-candidate-result evaluations both require replayable information boundaries, supporting this anchor. short_definitions.zh: 依据一个或多个评分、约束与预算为候选建立顺序或选择子集的活动。
short_definitions.en: An activity ordering candidates or selecting a subset under one or more scores, constraints, and budgets.
short_definitions.ja: 一つ以上のスコア・制約・予算下で候補を順序付け／部分集合選択する活動です。

definitions.zh: RankingOperation 是具有输入 CandidateSet、评分证据、策略、输出顺序或选择、时间和结果的活动，覆盖 RankFusion 与 TopKSelection；它不包含评分值、候选信息或检索查询本身。
definitions.en: A RankingOperation is an activity with input CandidateSet, score evidence, policy, output ordering or selection, time, and result. It covers RankFusion and TopKSelection and excludes score values, candidate information, and the retrieval query itself.
definitions.ja: RankingOperation は入力 CandidateSet・スコア証拠・方針・出力順序／選択・時刻・結果を持ち、RankFusion・TopKSelection を含みます。スコア値・候補情報・検索問い合わせ自体は除外します。

why_needed.zh: 排序操作为canonical 事实 RankFusion-is_a-RankingOperation（RankFusion is_a RankingOperation）提供明确端点并保持与 RankFusion 的定义边界。
why_needed.en: ranking operation provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact RankFusion-is_a-RankingOperation (RankFusion is_a RankingOperation) while preserving its reviewed boundary from RankFusion.

definitions.en: isolation and lookup boundary that separates memory records by user, task, project, tenant, agent, thread, or cross-session reuse context.
definitions.zh: 记忆命名空间是隔离和查找边界，用用户、任务、项目、租户、智能体、线程或跨会话复用上下文分隔记忆记录。
definitions.ja: 記憶名前空間は、ユーザー、タスク、プロジェクト、テナント、エージェント、スレッド、クロスセッション再利用文脈ごとに記憶レコードを分ける隔離と検索の境界です。

short_definitions.zh: 记忆记录是持久或会话级记忆条目，包含内容、来源、类型、命名空间、作用范围、保留状态、置信度和生命周期历史。
short_definitions.en: persistent or session-scoped memory entry with content, provenance, type, namespace, scope, retention state, confidence, and lifecycle history.
short_definitions.ja: 記憶レコードは、内容、来歴、型、名前空間、スコープ、保持状態、信頼度、ライフサイクル履歴を持つ永続またはセッション単位の記憶項目です。

definitions.en: persistent or session-scoped memory entry with content, provenance, type, namespace, scope, retention state, confidence, and lifecycle history.
definitions.zh: 记忆记录是持久或会话级记忆条目，包含内容、来源、类型、命名空间、作用范围、保留状态、置信度和生命周期历史。
definitions.ja: 記憶レコードは、内容、来歴、型、名前空間、スコープ、保持状態、信頼度、ライフサイクル履歴を持つ永続またはセッション単位の記憶項目です。

why_needed.ja: 記憶レコードはcanonical 事実 memory_record_belongs_to_data_zone（MemoryRecord memory_record_belongs_to_data_zone DataZone）の明確な端点を提供し、MemoryFile との審査済み境界を保ちながら。ノード項目 memory_scope、memory_kind、memory_record_id によりその区別を検証できます。

short_definitions.en: policy that governs how long memory records, summaries, indexes, and traces may be retained, refreshed, compacted, expired, deleted, or disclosed.
short_definitions.ja: 記憶保持ポリシーは、記憶レコード、要約、索引、トレースをどれだけ保持し、どのように更新、圧縮、期限切れ、削除、開示するかを統制します。

definitions.en: policy that governs how long memory records, summaries, indexes, and traces may be retained, refreshed, compacted, expired, deleted, or disclosed.
definitions.zh: 记忆保留策略治理记忆记录、摘要、索引和轨迹可以保留、刷新、压缩、过期、删除或披露多久以及如何处理。
definitions.ja: 記憶保持ポリシーは、記憶レコード、要約、索引、トレースをどれだけ保持し、どのように更新、圧縮、期限切れ、削除、開示するかを統制します。

why_needed.ja: 記憶保持方針はcanonical 事実 memory_store_governed_by_retention_policy（MemoryStore memory_store_governed_by_retention_policy MemoryRetentionPolicy）の明確な端点を提供し、MemoryStore との審査済み境界を保ちながら。

short_definitions.zh: 记忆范围是策略边界，说明记忆在会话、用户、任务、租户和信任上下文中可被读取、写入、更新、复用、披露或遗忘的位置。
short_definitions.en: policy boundary that states where memory may be read, written, updated, reused, disclosed, or forgotten across session, user, task, tenant, and trust contexts.
short_definitions.ja: 記憶スコープは、セッション、ユーザー、タスク、テナント、信頼文脈において記憶がどこで読み取り、書き込み、更新、再利用、開示、忘却され得るかを示す方針境界です。

definitions.en: policy boundary that states where memory may be read, written, updated, reused, disclosed, or forgotten across session, user, task, tenant, and trust contexts.
definitions.zh: 记忆范围是策略边界，说明记忆在会话、用户、任务、租户和信任上下文中可被读取、写入、更新、复用、披露或遗忘的位置。
definitions.ja: 記憶スコープは、セッション、ユーザー、タスク、テナント、信頼文脈において記憶がどこで読み取り、書き込み、更新、再利用、開示、忘却され得るかを示す方針境界です。

why_needed.zh: 记忆范围规范为canonical 事实 MemoryRecord-scoped_by-MemoryScope（MemoryRecord scoped_by MemoryScope）提供明确端点并保持与 MemoryRecord 的定义边界。
why_needed.en: memory scope provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact MemoryRecord-scoped_by-MemoryScope (MemoryRecord scoped_by MemoryScope) while preserving its reviewed boundary from MemoryRecord.
why_needed.ja: 記憶範囲仕様はcanonical 事実 MemoryRecord-scoped_by-MemoryScope（MemoryRecord scoped_by MemoryScope）の明確な端点を提供し、MemoryRecord との審査済み境界を保ちながら。

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of MemoryScope: MemoryScope is a governance specification for actors, tasks, sessions, tenants, and trust contexts in which records may be read, written, or reused; record scope classification is stored separately as a controlled field. The ontology-specific boundary for MemoryScope is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.

short_definitions.zh: 记忆库是存储锚点，支持在会话或长期范围内读取、写入、更新、删除、合并、压缩、过期、检索和审计记忆记录。
short_definitions.en: storage anchor that supports reading, writing, updating, deleting, merging, compacting, expiring, retrieving, and auditing memory records across session or long-term scopes.
short_definitions.ja: 記憶ストアは、セッションまたは長期スコープにまたがる記憶レコードの読み取り、書き込み、更新、削除、統合、圧縮、期限切れ、検索、監査を支える保存アンカーです。

definitions.en: storage anchor that supports reading, writing, updating, deleting, merging, compacting, expiring, retrieving, and auditing memory records across session or long-term scopes.
definitions.zh: 记忆库是存储锚点，支持在会话或长期范围内读取、写入、更新、删除、合并、压缩、过期、检索和审计记忆记录。
definitions.ja: 記憶ストアは、セッションまたは長期スコープにまたがる記憶レコードの読み取り、書き込み、更新、削除、統合、圧縮、期限切れ、検索、監査を支える保存アンカーです。

why_needed.zh: 记忆存储为canonical 事实 memory_store_governed_by_retention_policy（MemoryStore memory_store_governed_by_retention_policy MemoryRetentionPolicy）提供明确端点并保持与 MemoryRetentionPolicy 的定义边界。
why_needed.en: memory store provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact memory_store_governed_by_retention_policy (MemoryStore memory_store_governed_by_retention_policy MemoryRetentionPolicy) while preserving its reviewed boundary from MemoryRetentionPolicy.
why_needed.ja: 記憶ストアはcanonical 事実 memory_store_governed_by_retention_policy（MemoryStore memory_store_governed_by_retention_policy MemoryRetentionPolicy）の明確な端点を提供し、MemoryRetentionPolicy との審査済み境界を保ちながら。

The ontology-specific boundary for MemoryStore is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of MemoryStore: MemoryStore is a storage entity supporting record read, write, retrieval, deletion, compaction, expiration, and audit, not a MemoryRecord kind. The ontology-specific boundary for MemoryStore is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.

short_definitions.zh: 可被稳定寻址、存储、检索、引用并参与记忆生命周期的信息实体。
short_definitions.en: An information entity that can be stably addressed, stored, retrieved, referenced, and governed through a memory lifecycle.
short_definitions.ja: 安定アドレス指定・保存・検索・参照・記憶ライフサイクル統治が可能な情報実体です。

definitions.zh: MemoryEntity 是具有 canonical 身份、内容或载体、来源、版本和生命周期的信息实体，覆盖 MemoryRecord、MemoryItem 和 MemoryFile；kind、duration、scope 作为其记录字段的正交分类，不成为本类下的并列伪子类。MemoryStore、Namespace、Scope 和 Policy 通过关系治理它。
definitions.en: A MemoryEntity is an information entity with canonical identity, content or carrier, provenance, version, and lifecycle. It covers MemoryRecord, MemoryItem, and MemoryFile. Kind, duration, and scope are orthogonal record-field classifications rather than sibling pseudo-subclasses. MemoryStore, Namespace, Scope, and Policy govern it through relations.
definitions.ja: MemoryEntity は canonical 識別・内容／媒体・来歴・版・ライフサイクルを持つ情報実体で、MemoryRecord・MemoryItem・MemoryFile を含みます。kind・duration・scope はレコードフィールドの直交分類で並列偽下位型にしません。MemoryStore・Namespace・Scope・Policy は関係で統治します。

why_needed.zh: 记忆实体为canonical 事实 MemoryFile-is_a-MemoryEntity（MemoryFile is_a MemoryEntity）提供明确端点并保持与 MemoryFile 的定义边界。
why_needed.en: memory entity provides an explicit endpoint for canonical fact MemoryFile-is_a-MemoryEntity (MemoryFile is_a MemoryEntity) while preserving its reviewed boundary from MemoryFile.
why_needed.ja: 記憶実体はcanonical 事実 MemoryFile-is_a-MemoryEntity（MemoryFile is_a MemoryEntity）の明確な端点を提供し、MemoryFile との審査済み境界を保ちながら。

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of MemoryEntity: Agentic Memory's managed memory units and MemoryAgentBench's queryable, updatable, and forgettable objects jointly show that memory entities require stable identity and lifecycle. The ontology-specific boundary for MemoryEntity is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Paper > Agentic Memory; abstract and method or system-architecture sections

source_claims.supports: Provides evidence for the reviewed distinction of MemoryEntity: Agentic Memory's managed memory units and MemoryAgentBench's queryable, updatable, and forgettable objects jointly show that memory entities require stable identity and lifecycle. The ontology-specific boundary for MemoryEntity is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: README > MemoryAgentBench overview, architecture, and documented interfaces

definitions.zh: 输出观测关联独立的退出状态观测，使文本输出与终止状态不混为一个字段。
definitions.en: The output observation links to a distinct exit-status observation so textual output and termination status are not conflated.
definitions.ja: 出力観測は独立した終了状態観測へ結び、テキスト出力と終了状態を混同しません。

source_claims.supports: POSIX specifies command execution, standard output and standard error streams, and command exit-status semantics; Moonweave maps those domain constructs to the reviewed observation relation.
source_claims.locator: POSIX.1-2024 Shell Command Language > 2.1 Command Language Introduction; 2.8.2 Exit Status for Commands

definitions.zh: 命令输出观测 是一种 执行观测。区分特征：任一命令输出观测都是对执行所产生可见输出的 ExecutionObservation，区分特征是观测对象为命令输出。
definitions.en: command output observation is a kind of execution observation. Reviewed differentia: Every CommandOutputObservation is an ExecutionObservation whose observed subject is visible command output.
definitions.ja: コマンド出力観測 は 実行観測 の一種です。審査済みの種差：すべての CommandOutputObservation はコマンドの可視出力を対象とする ExecutionObservation です。

source_claims.supports: Supports the reviewed is_a relation from CommandOutputObservation to ExecutionObservation. The ontology-specific boundary for CommandOutputObservation-is_a-ExecutionObservation is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Specification > Architecture, base protocol, and server features

source_claims.supports: Supports the reviewed is_a relation from CommandOutputObservation to ExecutionObservation. The ontology-specific boundary for CommandOutputObservation-is_a-ExecutionObservation is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation page > Deep Agents JS docs heading and documented conceptual model

definitions.zh: 命令输出观测必须指出产生该可见输出的执行结果引用。
definitions.en: A command-output observation identifies the execution-result reference that produced the visible output.
definitions.ja: コマンド出力観測は可視出力を生成した実行結果参照を示します。

source_claims.supports: Supports the reviewed references_execution_result relation from CommandOutputObservation to ExecutionResultReference. The ontology-specific boundary for CommandOutputObservation-references_execution_result-ExecutionResultReference is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Specification > Architecture, base protocol, and server features

source_claims.supports: Supports the reviewed references_execution_result relation from CommandOutputObservation to ExecutionResultReference. The ontology-specific boundary for CommandOutputObservation-references_execution_result-ExecutionResultReference is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation page > Deep Agents JS docs heading and documented conceptual model

definitions.zh: 上下文摄入事件使经选择的命令输出观测对后续模型调用可见。
definitions.en: A context-ingress event makes a selected command-output observation available to a later model call.
definitions.ja: コンテキスト流入イベントは選択済みコマンド出力観測を後続モデル呼出しで利用可能にします。

source_claims.supports: The COMPASS study provides peer-reviewed evidence for selecting and compressing information supplied as an agent context; Moonweave defines the reviewed graph predicate and endpoint direction.
source_claims.locator: COMPASS paper > context construction and summarization method

definitions.en: links a context observation to the observable runtime trace event that produced it.
definitions.zh: 将上下文观测连接到产生该观测的可观察运行时追踪事件。
definitions.ja: コンテキスト観測を、その観測を生成した観測可能なランタイム追跡イベントへ結び付けます。

source_claims.supports: Supports the reviewed derived_from_trace_event relation from CommandOutputObservation to TraceEvent. The ontology-specific boundary for derived_from_trace_event is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Specification > Architecture, base protocol, and server features

source_claims.supports: Supports the reviewed derived_from_trace_event relation from CommandOutputObservation to TraceEvent. The ontology-specific boundary for derived_from_trace_event is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation page > OpenAI tools docs heading and documented conceptual model

definitions.zh: 环境绑定引用 是一种 执行引用。区分特征：任一环境绑定引用都是指向执行上下文绑定的 ExecutionReference，且只披露引用而非秘密值。
definitions.en: environment binding reference is a kind of execution reference. Reviewed differentia: Every EnvironmentBindingReference is an ExecutionReference to an execution-context binding and exposes the reference rather than secret values.
definitions.ja: 環境バインディング参照 は 実行参照 の一種です。審査済みの種差：すべての EnvironmentBindingReference は実行文脈バインディングを指す ExecutionReference で、秘密値ではなく参照だけを公開します。

source_claims.supports: Supports the reviewed is_a relation from EnvironmentBindingReference to ExecutionReference. The ontology-specific boundary for EnvironmentBindingReference-is_a-ExecutionReference is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Specification > Architecture, base protocol, and server features

source_claims.supports: Supports the reviewed is_a relation from EnvironmentBindingReference to ExecutionReference. The ontology-specific boundary for EnvironmentBindingReference-is_a-ExecutionReference is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation page > Deep Agents JS docs heading and documented conceptual model

definitions.zh: 执行观测以一个可审计的执行结果为观测对象；观测记录与被观测结果保持身份分离。
definitions.en: An execution observation observes an auditable execution result while keeping the observation record distinct from the observed result.
definitions.ja: 実行観測は監査可能な実行結果を観測対象とし、観測記録と観測対象結果の同一性を分離して保持します。

source_claims.supports: Supports the reviewed observes relation from ExecutionObservation to ExecutionResult. The ontology-specific boundary for ExecutionObservation-observes-ExecutionResult is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Specification > Architecture, base protocol, and server features

source_claims.supports: Supports the reviewed observes relation from ExecutionObservation to ExecutionResult. The ontology-specific boundary for ExecutionObservation-observes-ExecutionResult is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Tracing guide > Traces, spans, and trace processors

definitions.zh: 执行结果引用 是一种 执行引用。区分特征：任一执行结果引用都是以某次运行或工具结果为指称对象的 ExecutionReference。
definitions.en: execution result reference is a kind of execution reference. Reviewed differentia: Every ExecutionResultReference is an ExecutionReference whose referent is a runtime or tool result.
definitions.ja: 実行結果参照 は 実行参照 の一種です。審査済みの種差：すべての ExecutionResultReference は実行時またはツール結果を指示対象とする ExecutionReference です。

source_claims.supports: Supports the reviewed is_a relation from ExecutionResultReference to ExecutionReference. The ontology-specific boundary for ExecutionResultReference-is_a-ExecutionReference is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.

definitions.zh: 会话 是一种 会话结构元素。区分特征：任一会话都是按共同会话身份组织轮次和消息的 ConversationElement，区分特征是其聚合范围覆盖完整交换。

definitions.ja: 会話ターン は 会話構造要素 の一種です。審査済みの種差：すべての ConversationTurn は一つの交換位置を特定しメッセージを含む ConversationElement です。

definitions.zh: 外部智能体消息 是一种 消息。区分特征：外部智能体消息是一种跨互操作或信任边界交换的 Message，不是 Actor；修正旧 actor_type 并纳入消息层级。

definitions.en: links an instruction to the authority source used to resolve conflicts among system, developer, user, policy, retrieved, or tool-originated directives.
definitions.zh: 将指令连接到用于解决系统、开发者、用户、策略、检索内容或工具来源指令冲突的权威来源。

definitions.zh: 将可见输出中的引用连接到支持所显示主张或片段的精确来源区段。
definitions.ja: 可視出力の引用を、表示された主張またはセグメントを裏付ける正確な出典範囲へ結び付けます。

source_claims.supports: PROV-O defines provenance entities and relations for derivation, attribution, specialization, and source-bearing records; Moonweave defines the reviewed source-reference predicate and endpoint direction.
source_claims.locator: PROV-O Recommendation > Classes and Properties Defined in PROV-O

definitions.en: links a visible context window to the trust boundary it crosses when content from another actor, zone, tool, memory, or remote agent becomes visible.
definitions.zh: 该关系把可见上下文窗口连接到其跨越的信任边界，表示来自其它参与者、数据区、工具、记忆或远程智能体的内容变为可见。
definitions.ja: この関係は、別の参加者、データ区域、ツール、メモリ、遠隔エージェントからの内容が可視になるとき、可視コンテキストウィンドウを越える信頼境界に結びます。

source_claims.supports: Supports the reviewed context_window_crosses_trust_boundary relation from VisibleContextWindow to TrustBoundary. The ontology-specific boundary for context_window_crosses_trust_boundary is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation page > OpenAI guardrails docs heading and documented conceptual model

source_claims.supports: Supports the reviewed context_window_crosses_trust_boundary relation from VisibleContextWindow to TrustBoundary. The ontology-specific boundary for context_window_crosses_trust_boundary is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Tracing guide > Traces, spans, and trace processors

definitions.zh: 上下文选择决定记录采用哪个窗口及采用理由。
definitions.en: A context-selection decision records which window was chosen and why.
definitions.ja: コンテキスト選択判断は採用したウィンドウと理由を記録します。

source_claims.supports: Supports the reviewed selects_window relation from ContextSelectionDecision to OutputWindow. The ontology-specific boundary for ContextSelectionDecision-selects_window-OutputWindow is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Specification > Architecture, base protocol, and server features

source_claims.supports: Supports the reviewed selects_window relation from ContextSelectionDecision to OutputWindow. The ontology-specific boundary for ContextSelectionDecision-selects_window-OutputWindow is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation page > Deep Agents JS docs heading and documented conceptual model

definitions.zh: 选择决定明确关联因策略、预算或相关性而被抑制的内容记录。
definitions.en: The selection decision explicitly links the content record suppressed for policy, budget, or relevance reasons.
definitions.ja: 選択判断は方針・予算・関連性により抑制された内容記録へ明示的に結び付きます。

source_claims.supports: Supports the reviewed suppresses relation from ContextSelectionDecision to SuppressedContext. The ontology-specific boundary for ContextSelectionDecision-suppresses-SuppressedContext is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Specification > Architecture, base protocol, and server features

source_claims.supports: Supports the reviewed suppresses relation from ContextSelectionDecision to SuppressedContext. The ontology-specific boundary for ContextSelectionDecision-suppresses-SuppressedContext is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation page > Deep Agents JS docs heading and documented conceptual model

definitions.zh: 已披露输出片段 是一种 披露审计制品。区分特征：任一已披露输出片段既是披露控制产生的 DisclosureArtifact，也仍是具有顺序和来源的 OutputSegment；多父维度分别表达治理状态和内容结构。
definitions.en: disclosed output segment is a kind of disclosure audit artifact. Reviewed differentia: Every DisclosedOutputSegment is both a DisclosureArtifact produced by disclosure control and an OutputSegment retaining order and source; the two parents capture governance status and content structure.
definitions.ja: 開示済み出力区間 は 開示監査成果物 の一種です。審査済みの種差：すべての DisclosedOutputSegment は開示制御が生成する DisclosureArtifact であり、順序と出典を持つ OutputSegment でもあり、二父は統治状態と内容構造を表します。

source_claims.supports: Supports the reviewed is_a relation from DisclosedOutputSegment to DisclosureArtifact. The ontology-specific boundary for DisclosedOutputSegment-is_a-DisclosureArtifact is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Specification > Architecture, base protocol, and server features

source_claims.supports: Supports the reviewed is_a relation from DisclosedOutputSegment to DisclosureArtifact. The ontology-specific boundary for DisclosedOutputSegment-is_a-DisclosureArtifact is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation page > Deep Agents JS docs heading and documented conceptual model

definitions.zh: 已披露输出片段 是一种 输出片段。区分特征：任一已披露输出片段既是披露控制产生的 DisclosureArtifact，也仍是具有顺序和来源的 OutputSegment；多父维度分别表达治理状态和内容结构。
definitions.en: disclosed output segment is a kind of output segment. Reviewed differentia: Every DisclosedOutputSegment is both a DisclosureArtifact produced by disclosure control and an OutputSegment retaining order and source; the two parents capture governance status and content structure.
definitions.ja: 開示済み出力区間 は 出力区間 の一種です。審査済みの種差：すべての DisclosedOutputSegment は開示制御が生成する DisclosureArtifact であり、順序と出典を持つ OutputSegment でもあり、二父は統治状態と内容構造を表します。

source_claims.supports: Supports the reviewed is_a relation from DisclosedOutputSegment to OutputSegment. The ontology-specific boundary for DisclosedOutputSegment-is_a-OutputSegment is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Specification > Architecture, base protocol, and server features

source_claims.supports: Supports the reviewed is_a relation from DisclosedOutputSegment to OutputSegment. The ontology-specific boundary for DisclosedOutputSegment-is_a-OutputSegment is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation page > Deep Agents JS docs heading and documented conceptual model

definitions.zh: 输出披露活动引用 Safety 域唯一拥有的渐进披露控制，不在 Info 域复制。
definitions.en: Output-disclosure activity references the ProgressiveDisclosure control uniquely owned by Safety rather than duplicating it in Info.
definitions.ja: 出力開示活動は Info で複製せず、Safety が一意に所有する ProgressiveDisclosure 制御を参照します。

source_claims.supports: Supports the reviewed governed_by relation from DisclosureStage to ProgressiveDisclosure. The ontology-specific boundary for DisclosureStage-governed_by-ProgressiveDisclosure is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Specification > Architecture, base protocol, and server features

source_claims.supports: Supports the reviewed governed_by relation from DisclosureStage to ProgressiveDisclosure. The ontology-specific boundary for DisclosureStage-governed_by-ProgressiveDisclosure is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation page > Deep Agents JS docs heading and documented conceptual model

definitions.zh: 披露阶段产生带接收方、边界和引用信息的可见输出片段。
definitions.en: A disclosure stage produces a visible output segment with recipient, boundary, and citation information.
definitions.ja: 開示段階は受信者・境界・引用情報付き可視出力区間を生成します。

source_claims.supports: Supports the reviewed produces relation from DisclosureStage to DisclosedOutputSegment. The ontology-specific boundary for DisclosureStage-produces-DisclosedOutputSegment is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Specification > Architecture, base protocol, and server features

source_claims.supports: Supports the reviewed produces relation from DisclosureStage to DisclosedOutputSegment. The ontology-specific boundary for DisclosureStage-produces-DisclosedOutputSegment is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation page > Deep Agents JS docs heading and documented conceptual model

definitions.en: links a disclosed output segment to the actor or recipient that can see it.
definitions.zh: 将已披露输出片段连接到能够查看它的行为者或接收者。
definitions.ja: 開示済み出力セグメントを、それを閲覧できるアクターまたは受信者へ結び付けます。

source_claims.supports: NIST SP 800-162 defines access decisions over subject, object, operation, and environment attributes; Moonweave maps the resulting authorization boundary to the reviewed display or release relation.
source_claims.locator: NIST SP 800-162 > Attribute Based Access Control definition and functional architecture

definitions.zh: 首尾输出窗口 是一种 输出窗口。区分特征：任一首尾窗口都是通过保留开头和结尾范围定义可见区间的 OutputWindow。
definitions.en: head-tail output window is a kind of output window. Reviewed differentia: Every HeadTailWindow is an OutputWindow whose visible range retains the beginning and ending portions.
definitions.ja: 先頭末尾出力ウィンドウ は 出力ウィンドウ の一種です。審査済みの種差：すべての HeadTailWindow は先頭と末尾範囲を保持して可視区間を定める OutputWindow です。

source_claims.supports: Supports the reviewed is_a relation from HeadTailWindow to OutputWindow. The ontology-specific boundary for HeadTailWindow-is_a-OutputWindow is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Specification > Architecture, base protocol, and server features

source_claims.supports: Supports the reviewed is_a relation from HeadTailWindow to OutputWindow. The ontology-specific boundary for HeadTailWindow-is_a-OutputWindow is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation page > Deep Agents JS docs heading and documented conceptual model

definitions.en: links an output segment to the sensitive span requiring redaction, masking, suppression, delayed release, or restricted routing.
definitions.zh: 该关系把输出片段连接到需要脱敏、掩码、抑制、延迟释放或限制路由的敏感片段。
definitions.ja: この関係は、出力セグメントを、秘匿、マスク、抑制、遅延解放、経路制限が必要な機微範囲に結びます。

source_claims.supports: Supports the reviewed output_segment_has_sensitive_span relation from OutputSegment to SensitiveSpan. The ontology-specific boundary for output_segment_has_sensitive_span is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation page > OpenAI guardrails docs heading and documented conceptual model

source_claims.supports: Supports the reviewed output_segment_has_sensitive_span relation from OutputSegment to SensitiveSpan. The ontology-specific boundary for output_segment_has_sensitive_span is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Tracing guide > Traces, spans, and trace processors

definitions.zh: 输出分块 是一种 输出片段。区分特征：任一输出分块都是为传输、引用或预算而划定边界的 OutputSegment。
definitions.en: output chunk is a kind of output segment. Reviewed differentia: Every OutputChunk is an OutputSegment bounded for transport, citation, or budgeting.
definitions.ja: 出力チャンク は 出力区間 の一種です。審査済みの種差：すべての OutputChunk は転送・引用・予算管理用に境界付けた OutputSegment です。

source_claims.supports: Supports the reviewed is_a relation from OutputChunk to OutputSegment. The ontology-specific boundary for OutputChunk-is_a-OutputSegment is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.

definitions.zh: 输出引用通过稳定引用锚定位到支持可见片段的原始来源。
definitions.en: An output citation uses a stable citation anchor to locate the original source supporting the visible segment.
definitions.ja: 出力引用は安定引用アンカーで可視区間を支える原出典を特定します。

source_claims.supports: Supports the reviewed anchored_by relation from OutputCitation to CitationAnchor. The ontology-specific boundary for OutputCitation-anchored_by-CitationAnchor is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Specification > Architecture, base protocol, and server features

source_claims.supports: Supports the reviewed anchored_by relation from OutputCitation to CitationAnchor. The ontology-specific boundary for OutputCitation-anchored_by-CitationAnchor is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation page > Deep Agents JS docs heading and documented conceptual model

definitions.zh: 输出引用 是一种 输出信息。区分特征：任一输出引用都是把可见输出关联到来源锚的 OutputInformation，区分特征是证据定位。
definitions.en: output citation is a kind of output information. Reviewed differentia: Every OutputCitation is OutputInformation linking visible output to a source anchor, distinguished by evidence location.
definitions.ja: 出力引用 は 出力情報 の一種です。審査済みの種差：すべての OutputCitation は可視出力を出典アンカーへ結ぶ OutputInformation で、証拠位置により区別されます。

source_claims.supports: Supports the reviewed is_a relation from OutputCitation to OutputInformation. The ontology-specific boundary for OutputCitation-is_a-OutputInformation is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Specification > Architecture, base protocol, and server features

source_claims.supports: Supports the reviewed is_a relation from OutputCitation to OutputInformation. The ontology-specific boundary for OutputCitation-is_a-OutputInformation is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation page > Deep Agents JS docs heading and documented conceptual model

definitions.zh: 输出片段 是一种 输出信息。区分特征：任一输出片段都是具有顺序、范围、来源和可见性的 OutputInformation。
definitions.en: output segment is a kind of output information. Reviewed differentia: Every OutputSegment is OutputInformation with order, extent, source, and visibility.
definitions.ja: 出力区間 は 出力情報 の一種です。審査済みの種差：すべての OutputSegment は順序・範囲・出典・可視性を持つ OutputInformation です。

source_claims.supports: Supports the reviewed is_a relation from OutputSegment to OutputInformation. The ontology-specific boundary for OutputSegment-is_a-OutputInformation is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.

source_claims.supports: Supports the reviewed is_a relation from OutputSegment to OutputInformation. The ontology-specific boundary for OutputSegment-is_a-OutputInformation is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation page > Deep Agents JS docs heading and documented conceptual model

definitions.zh: 输出片段可进一步划分为用于传输、引用或预算的分块。
definitions.en: An output segment may be partitioned into chunks for transport, citation, or budgeting.
definitions.ja: 出力区間は転送・引用・予算管理用チャンクへさらに分割できます。

source_claims.supports: Supports the reviewed partitioned_into relation from OutputSegment to OutputChunk. The ontology-specific boundary for OutputSegment-partitioned_into-OutputChunk is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Specification > Architecture, base protocol, and server features

source_claims.supports: Supports the reviewed partitioned_into relation from OutputSegment to OutputChunk. The ontology-specific boundary for OutputSegment-partitioned_into-OutputChunk is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.

definitions.zh: 输出流由有序、可寻址的输出片段组成。
definitions.en: An output stream is composed of ordered, addressable output segments.
definitions.ja: 出力ストリームは順序付きでアドレス可能な出力区間から構成されます。

definitions.en: output stream is a kind of output information. Reviewed differentia: Every OutputStream is a collection of OutputInformation segments ordered by production.
definitions.ja: 出力ストリーム は 出力情報 の一種です。審査済みの種差：すべての OutputStream は生成順に出力区間をまとめる OutputInformation 集合です。

source_claims.supports: Supports the reviewed is_a relation from OutputStream to OutputInformation. The ontology-specific boundary for OutputStream-is_a-OutputInformation is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.

source_claims.locator: Documentation page > Deep Agents JS docs heading and documented conceptual model

definitions.zh: 输出窗口 是一种 输出信息。区分特征：任一输出窗口都是规定某接收方可见或保留范围的 OutputInformation 规范。
definitions.en: output window is a kind of output information. Reviewed differentia: Every OutputWindow is an OutputInformation specification defining the range visible or retained for a recipient.
definitions.ja: 出力ウィンドウ は 出力情報 の一種です。審査済みの種差：すべての OutputWindow は受信者に可視または保持する範囲を定める OutputInformation 仕様です。

source_claims.supports: Supports the reviewed is_a relation from OutputWindow to OutputInformation. The ontology-specific boundary for OutputWindow-is_a-OutputInformation is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Specification > Architecture, base protocol, and server features

source_claims.supports: Supports the reviewed is_a relation from OutputWindow to OutputInformation. The ontology-specific boundary for OutputWindow-is_a-OutputInformation is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.

definitions.zh: 输出窗口声明从哪个输出流选择可见范围。
definitions.en: An output window states the output stream from which its visible range is selected.
definitions.ja: 出力ウィンドウは可視範囲を選択する元の出力ストリームを示します。

definitions.en: links a disclosed output segment to the trust boundary or external boundary it is allowed to cross.
definitions.zh: 将已披露输出片段连接到获准跨越的信任边界或外部边界。
definitions.ja: 開示済み出力セグメントを、通過を許可された信頼境界または外部境界へ結び付けます。

source_claims.supports: NIST SP 800-162 defines access decisions over subject, object, operation, and environment attributes; Moonweave maps the resulting authorization boundary to the reviewed display or release relation.
source_claims.locator: NIST SP 800-162 > Attribute Based Access Control definition and functional architecture

definitions.en: links suppressed context or output to the disclosure, redaction, privacy, safety, or boundary rule that withheld it.
definitions.zh: 将被抑制的上下文或输出连接到扣留它的披露、脱敏、隐私、安全或边界规则。
definitions.ja: 抑止されたコンテキストまたは出力を、それを留保した開示、墨消し、プライバシー、安全、または境界ルールへ結び付けます。

source_claims.supports: OPA documents policy evaluation over structured input and data with structured decisions; Moonweave maps that policy decision to the reviewed redaction or suppression relation.
source_claims.locator: OPA Documentation > Policy Language and How OPA Works

definitions.zh: 已抑制上下文 是一种 披露审计制品。区分特征：任一已抑制上下文都是记录因策略、隐私、相关性或预算未披露内容的 DisclosureArtifact。
definitions.en: suppressed context is a kind of disclosure audit artifact. Reviewed differentia: Every SuppressedContext is a DisclosureArtifact recording content withheld for policy, privacy, relevance, or budget reasons.
definitions.ja: 抑制済みコンテキスト は 開示監査成果物 の一種です。審査済みの種差：すべての SuppressedContext は方針・プライバシー・関連性・予算理由で非開示となった内容を記録する DisclosureArtifact です。

source_claims.supports: Supports the reviewed is_a relation from SuppressedContext to DisclosureArtifact. The ontology-specific boundary for SuppressedContext-is_a-DisclosureArtifact is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Specification > Architecture, base protocol, and server features

source_claims.supports: Supports the reviewed is_a relation from SuppressedContext to DisclosureArtifact. The ontology-specific boundary for SuppressedContext-is_a-DisclosureArtifact is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.

definitions.zh: 已截断上下文片段 是一种 披露审计制品。区分特征：任一截断上下文片段都是记录窗口、限长或摘要移除范围的 DisclosureArtifact，而不是长度值。
definitions.en: truncated context span is a kind of disclosure audit artifact. Reviewed differentia: Every TruncatedContextSpan is a DisclosureArtifact recording the extent removed by windowing, limits, or summarization, not a length value.
definitions.ja: 切詰め済みコンテキスト範囲 は 開示監査成果物 の一種です。審査済みの種差：すべての TruncatedContextSpan はウィンドウ・制限・要約で除去した範囲を記録する DisclosureArtifact で、長さ値ではありません。

source_claims.supports: Supports the reviewed is_a relation from TruncatedContextSpan to DisclosureArtifact. The ontology-specific boundary for TruncatedContextSpan-is_a-DisclosureArtifact is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Specification > Architecture, base protocol, and server features

source_claims.supports: Supports the reviewed is_a relation from TruncatedContextSpan to DisclosureArtifact. The ontology-specific boundary for TruncatedContextSpan-is_a-DisclosureArtifact is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation page > Deep Agents JS docs heading and documented conceptual model

definitions.zh: 截断片段记录它从哪个输出窗口中被移除，支持完整性审计。
definitions.en: A truncated span records the output window from which it was omitted for completeness auditing.
definitions.ja: 切詰め範囲は完全性監査のため、どの出力ウィンドウから除外されたかを記録します。

source_claims.supports: Supports the reviewed omitted_from relation from TruncatedContextSpan to OutputWindow. The ontology-specific boundary for TruncatedContextSpan-omitted_from-OutputWindow is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.

definitions.zh: 可见上下文窗口 是一种 输出窗口。区分特征：任一可见上下文窗口都是针对特定 Actor、模型调用或步骤定义可见范围的 OutputWindow。
definitions.en: visible context window is a kind of output window. Reviewed differentia: Every VisibleContextWindow is an OutputWindow defining the visible range for a specific Actor, model call, or step.
definitions.ja: 可視コンテキストウィンドウ は 出力ウィンドウ の一種です。審査済みの種差：すべての VisibleContextWindow は特定 Actor・モデル呼出し・ステップの可視範囲を定める OutputWindow です。

source_claims.supports: Supports the reviewed is_a relation from VisibleContextWindow to OutputWindow. The ontology-specific boundary for VisibleContextWindow-is_a-OutputWindow is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.

source_claims.supports: Supports the reviewed is_a relation from VisibleContextWindow to OutputWindow. The ontology-specific boundary for VisibleContextWindow-is_a-OutputWindow is an accepted design inference over this registered evidence.
source_claims.locator: Documentation page > Deep Agents JS docs heading and documented conceptual model

definitions.en: links a source or context artifact to the data zone governing its visibility, retention, and allowed use.
definitions.zh: 该关系把来源或上下文产物连接到治理其可见性、保留期限和允许用途的数据区。
definitions.ja: この関係は、出典またはコンテキスト成果物を、その可視性、保持、許可用途を統制するデータ区域に結びます。
